Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. Eventbrite is the ticketing/payment backbone. Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge between both systems.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_order_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs a valid `event_id` to render\n- FR6 (Cancellation) — Eventbrite cancel API needs a valid event tied to a valid order\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking requires a confirmed Eventbrite order under that `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks. No tickets can be sold, no orders created, no refunds processed.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API on Eventbrite. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps are enforced in your internal DB. |\n| Venue creation/updates | No venue creation API. Venues can only be managed via Eventbrite Dashboard. Your app can read pre-existing venue IDs only. |\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API on Eventbrite. The entire state machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API on Eventbrite. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | No order creation API. Orders are created exclusively via the Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK). Backend only reads orders post-creation. |\n| Attendee creation | No attendee creation API. Attendees are created automatically by Checkout Widget when orders are placed. Backend only reads attendee records. |\n| Payment processing | Eventbrite Checkout Widget handles all payments (credit card, debit, UPI, Eventbrite wallet, PayPal, etc.). Backend reads orders post-creation via EbOrderService. |\n| Single order cancel | No per-order cancel API. Only full event cancel exists. Per-order cancellation is mimicked via org admin token. |\n| Refund submission | No programmatic refund API. Refund status is embedded in order `refund_request` field only. Read-only access. |\n| Webhook registration | No webhook API. Webhooks are registered via Eventbrite Dashboard only. This app simulates webhooks for testing/development. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API on Eventbrite. Entire review system is internal. Attendance verification uses attendee read API. |\n| Public event search | `GET /events/search/` is deprecated. Event discovery is limited to your org's events. |\n\n## 6. Functional Requirement Flows — Internal + Eventbrite Sync\n\n### FR1 — City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n**Module:** discovery-catalog\n\nThis flow provides the entry point for users browsing events. The internal catalog is the primary source. Eventbrite contributes only events your org has created — public city-wide search is not available (deprecated API).\n\n"
  }
}
```
---
## Entry 22
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:12:39.601Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:12:39.601Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_9uugK853mXqc6XfLfjc2Lbbb",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 30b239\nWall time: 0.0503 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3133\nOutput:\n# SPEC_3.MD — User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n## 1. Overview\nThis feature implements end-to-end onboarding for public users: registration, authentication, password flows, profile setup, preference selection, and wallet read. It is identity-only and uses shared JWT/security components.\n\n**Primary users:** Public users (`USER`) registering and onboarding\n**Secondary users:** Admin onboarding is out of scope for this flow\n**Tech stack:** Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21, JPA, Flyway, JWT (`shared/security`)\n**Scope:** v1.0 — User Onboarding (FR3 Stage 1)\n\n---\n\n## 2. Goals & Non-Goals\n\n### Goals\n- Enable registration + login with JWT access tokens and opaque refresh tokens.\n- Support authenticated password change and token-based password reset.\n- Support profile + preferences onboarding via a junction-backed preferences model (city FK + genre junction table).\n- Provide active preference options API and wallet read API.\n\n### Non-Goals (Out of Scope)\n- Email verification and MFA.\n- Password reset email delivery (token generation only).\n- Eventbrite sync during onboarding.\n- Admin registration/profile mapping in this flow.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Requirements\n\n### User Registration & Auth\n- [MUST] Allow registration with email + password; store password using BCrypt (never plaintext).\n- [MUST] Registration request accepts only `email` and `password`. Role is not exposed in the public API; server always sets `role = USER` internally.\n- [MUST] Duplicate email check is the **first validation** performed on registration, before all other checks.\n- [MUST] Registration request must reject unknown/extra fields (e.g., `role`) with `400 Bad Request`.\n- [MUST] Allow login with email + password and issue:\n  - Access JWT with 1 hour expiry.\n  - Refresh token with 1 day expiry.\n- [MUST] Provide refresh token exchange endpoint to issue a new access JWT.\n- [MUST] Provide logout endpoint that revokes refresh tokens.\n- [MUST] Secure all non-auth endpoints with JWT via `shared/security/JwtTokenProvider` + JWT auth filter (no Basic auth).\n- [MUST] Reject duplicate email registrations (case-insensitive).\n- [WONT] Email verification and MFA in this flow.\n\n### Password Management\n- [MUST] Provide authenticated password change with current + new + confirm.\n- [MUST] Provide password reset request endpoint that generates reset token.\n- [MUST] Provide password reset confirm endpoint that validates token and sets new password.\n- [MUST] Only most recently issued reset token is valid; issuing a new token invalidates prior active tokens.\n- [MUST] Store reset token as hash; enforce unexpired + unconsumed + latest-only checks.\n- [WONT] Email delivery implementation (token generation only).\n\n### Profile + Preferences\n- [MUST] Provide upsert API for user settings (profile + preferences).\n- [MUST] Provide API to fetch user settings.\n- [MUST] Preferences must be selected from global preference options stored in identity DB.\n- [MUST] Enforce exactly 1 city and up to 3 genres.\n- [SHOULD] Validate preference values against active options.\n\n### Preference Options\n- [MUST] Provide API to list active preference options (city + genre).\n\n### Wallet\n- [MUST] Provide API to read wallet balance (identity-owned source of truth).\n\n### Eventbrite Sync\n- [WONT] No Eventbrite sync in onboarding flow.\n\n### Non-Functional / Architecture\n- [MUST] Implement in `identity` module only, following `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`.\n- [MUST] Use `shared/` for JWT/security only; no external API calls in this flow.\n- [MUST] Follow test layering from `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Domain Model\n\nA `User` in this flow is a public account with role `USER`. User owns `UserSettings` (profile + preferences) and `UserWallet`. Preferences are selected from active global `PreferenceOption` entries. Refresh tokens and password reset tokens are persisted for session and recovery control.\n\n**Entities & Relationships**\n- `User`\n  - Unique `id`, unique case-insensitive `email`.\n  - Fields: `passwordHash` (BCrypt), `role` (default `USER`), `status` (default `ACTIVE`).\n  - 1:1 `UserSettings`, 1:1 `UserWallet`.\n\n- `UserSettings`\n  - Unique by `user_id`.\n  - Profile fields: `fullName`, `phone`, `dob`, `address`.\n  - `preferred_city_option_id` FK → `preference_options(id)` where `type = CITY`.\n  - Up to 3 genre preferences stored in `user_genre_preferences` junction table.\n  - No direct FK to `discovery-catalog`'s `city` table — `identity` owns its own city option list.\n\n- `UserWallet`\n  - Unique by `user_id`.\n  - Created at registration for `USER`. Initial balance = 0.\n\n- `PreferenceOption`\n  - `id BIGSERIAL`, type `CITY` or `GENRE`, value string, active flag, sort order.\n  - Identity-owned; independent of `discovery-catalog`'s `city` table (no transitive dependency).\n\n- `UserGenrePreference` (junction — full JPA entity with own PK)\n  - `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_settings_id FK → user_settings(id)`, `preference_option_id FK → preference_options(id)` where `type = GENRE`.\n  - Unique on `(user_settings_id, preference_option_id)`. Max 3 per `user_settings_id` enforced at service layer.\n\n- `RefreshToken`\n  - `user_id FK → users(id)`, `token_hash`, `expires_at`, optional `revoked_at`.\n  - Revocation is enforced during refresh.\n\n- `PasswordResetToken`\n  - `user_id FK → users(id)`, `token_hash`, `expires_at`, optional `consumed_at`.\n  - Latest-token-wins policy.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Architecture Pattern\n**Modular Monolith (Fixed)** — module registry in `PRODUCT.md`.\n\n### Module Ownership\n**Primary module:** `identity`  \n**Cross-module dependencies:** none\n\n### Inter-Module Communication\n| Interaction | Type | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| None | N/A | N/A |\n\n### Feature Files\n```\nidentity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/\n├── api/controller/AuthController.java\n├── api/controller/UserSettingsController.java\n├── api/controller/PreferenceOptionsController.java\n├── api/controller/UserWalletController.java\n├── api/dto/request/RegisterRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/LoginRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/RefreshTokenRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/LogoutRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/PasswordChangeRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/PasswordResetRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/PasswordResetConfirmRequest.java\n├── api/dto/request/UserSettingsUpsertRequest.java\n├── api/dto/response/RegisterResponse.java\n├── api/dto/response/AuthTokensResponse.java\n├── api/dto/response/UserProfileResponse.java\n├── api/dto/response/UserSettingsResponse.java\n├── api/dto/response/PreferenceOptionsResponse.java\n├── api/dto/response/UserWalletResponse.java\n├── domain/User.java\n├── domain/UserSettings.java\n├── domain/UserWallet.java\n├── domain/PreferenceOption.java\n├── domain/UserGenrePreference.java\n├── domain/RefreshToken.java\n├── domain/PasswordResetToken.java\n├── service/AuthService.java\n├── service/UserDetailsServiceImpl.java\n├── service/UserSettingsService.java\n├── service/PreferenceOptionsService.java\n├── service/UserWalletService.java\n├── repository/UserRepository.java\n├── repository/UserSettingsRepository.java\n├── repository/UserWalletRepository.java\n├── repository/PreferenceOptionRepository.java\n├── repository/RefreshTokenRepository.java\n├── repository/PasswordResetTokenRepository.java\n├── mapper/UserMapper.java\n├── mapper/UserSettingsMapper.java\n├── mapper/PreferenceOptionMapper.java\n├── mapper/UserWalletMapper.java\n└── exception/UserNotFoundException.java\n```\n\n### Shared Security Files (implemented — see SPEC_AUTH.MD)\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/\n├── JwtTokenProvider.java          ← RS256 sign, validate, extract claims (@Component)\n├── JwtAuthenticationFilter.java   ← OncePerRequestFilter; reads Bearer token, sets SecurityContext\n├── SecurityConfig.java            ← stateless JWT filter chain; PasswordEncoder + AuthManager beans\n├── SecurityConstants.java         ← single source of truth for all TTLs, header names, claim keys\n└── Roles.java                     ← USER / ADMIN string constants\n```\n\n> All token TTLs used by `AuthService` must be read from `SecurityConstants` (in `shared/security/`), not hardcoded:\n> - `SecurityConstants.ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_S` = 3600 (1 hour)\n> - `SecurityConstants.REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_S` = 86400 (1 day)\n> - `SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S` = 900 (15 minutes)\n>\n> `SecurityConfig.java` permits these FR3 public endpoints without JWT: `/api/v1/auth/register`, `/api/v1/auth/login`, `/api/v1/auth/token/refresh`, `/api/v1/auth/password/reset`, `/api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm`. All other endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.\n\n---\n\n## 6. DB Schema\n\n### Tables\n\n**`users`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`\n- `role VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USER'`, `status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE'`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique index on `LOWER(email)` (case-insensitive).\n- No `organization_id` — booking users have no org context in this flow.\n\n**`user_settings`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT UNIQUE FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `full_name VARCHAR(255)`, `phone VARCHAR(20)`, `dob DATE`, `address TEXT`\n- `preferred_city_option_id BIGINT FK → preference_options(id)` — must be `type = CITY`\n- `notification_opt_in BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique `user_id`.\n- Index: `idx_user_settings_city_option` on `preferred_city_option_id` (FK performance)\n\n**`user_wallets`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT UNIQUE FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `balance_amount NUMERIC(19,4) DEFAULT 0`, `currency VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'INR'`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique `user_id`.\n\n**`preference_options`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL` (`CITY` or `GENRE`)\n- `value VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL`, `active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE`, `sort_order INT DEFAULT 0`\n- Constraint: `UNIQUE (type, value)`\n- Indexes: `idx_preference_type` on `type`, `idx_preference_active` on `active`\n\n**`user_genre_preferences`** (junction — replaces `TEXT[]` approach)\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`\n- `user_settings_id BIGINT FK → user_settings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `preference_option_id BIGINT FK → preference_options(id)`\n- Constraint: `UNIQUE (user_settings_id, preference_option_id)`\n- Max 3 rows per `user_settings_id` enforced at service layer (not DB constraint).\n- Index: `idx_genre_pref_option_id` on `preference_option_id` (FK performance)\n\n**`refresh_tokens`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL`\n- `revoked_at TIMESTAMP`, `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Indexes: `idx_refresh_user_id` on `user_id`; `UNIQUE idx_refresh_token_hash` on `token_hash` (lookup on every refresh call)\n\n**`password_reset_tokens`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL`\n- `consumed_at TIMESTAMP`, `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Indexes: `idx_reset_user_id` on `user_id`; `UNIQUE idx_reset_token_hash` on `token_hash` (lookup on every reset confirm call)\n\n### Flyway Migrations\nUse **next global versions** in `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/` (single Flyway scan path).\n\nFR1 used V0–V4. FR2 used V5–V9 (highest: `V9__create_show_slot_pricing_tier_table.sql`). FR3 starts at V10:\n\n- `V10__create_users.sql` — users table\n- `V11__create_preference_options.sql` — preference_options table _(must exist before user_settings FK)_\n- `V12__create_user_settings.sql` — user_settings with `preferred_city_option_id FK`\n- `V13__create_user_genre_preferences.sql` — junction table\n- `V14__create_user_wallets.sql` — user_wallets\n- `V15__create_refresh_tokens.sql` — refresh_tokens\n- `V16__create_password_reset_tokens.sql` — password_reset_tokens\n\n> Note: `preference_options` must be created before `user_settings` due to the FK on `preferred_city_option_id`.\n\n"
  }
}
```

`/api/v1/auth/token/refresh`, `/api/v1/auth/password/reset`, `/api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm`. All other endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.\n\n---\n\n## 6. DB Schema\n\n### Tables\n\n**`users`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`\n- `role VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USER'`, `status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE'`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique index on `LOWER(email)` (case-insensitive).\n- No `organization_id` — booking users have no org context in this flow.\n\n**`user_settings`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT UNIQUE FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `full_name VARCHAR(255)`, `phone VARCHAR(20)`, `dob DATE`, `address TEXT`\n- `preferred_city_option_id BIGINT FK → preference_options(id)` — must be `type = CITY`\n- `notification_opt_in BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique `user_id`.\n- Index: `idx_user_settings_city_option` on `preferred_city_option_id` (FK performance)\n\n**`user_wallets`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT UNIQUE FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `balance_amount NUMERIC(19,4) DEFAULT 0`, `currency VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'INR'`\n- `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Constraint: unique `user_id`.\n\n**`preference_options`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL` (`CITY` or `GENRE`)\n- `value VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL`, `active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE`, `sort_order INT DEFAULT 0`\n- Constraint: `UNIQUE (type, value)`\n- Indexes: `idx_preference_type` on `type`, `idx_preference_active` on `active`\n\n**`user_genre_preferences`** (junction — replaces `TEXT[]` approach)\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`\n- `user_settings_id BIGINT FK → user_settings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `preference_option_id BIGINT FK → preference_options(id)`\n- Constraint: `UNIQUE (user_settings_id, preference_option_id)`\n- Max 3 rows per `user_settings_id` enforced at service layer (not DB constraint).\n- Index: `idx_genre_pref_option_id` on `preference_option_id` (FK performance)\n\n**`refresh_tokens`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL`\n- `revoked_at TIMESTAMP`, `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Indexes: `idx_refresh_user_id` on `user_id`; `UNIQUE idx_refresh_token_hash` on `token_hash` (lookup on every refresh call)\n\n**`password_reset_tokens`**\n- `id BIGSERIAL PK`, `user_id BIGINT FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE`\n- `token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL`, `expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL`\n- `consumed_at TIMESTAMP`, `created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`\n- Indexes: `idx_reset_user_id` on `user_id`; `UNIQUE idx_reset_token_hash` on `token_hash` (lookup on every reset confirm call)\n\n### Flyway Migrations\nUse **next global versions** in `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/` (single Flyway scan path).\n\nFR1 used V0–V4. FR2 used V5–V9 (highest: `V9__create_show_slot_pricing_tier_table.sql`). FR3 starts at V10:\n\n- `V10__create_users.sql` — users table\n- `V11__create_preference_options.sql` — preference_options table _(must exist before user_settings FK)_\n- `V12__create_user_settings.sql` — user_settings with `preferred_city_option_id FK`\n- `V13__create_user_genre_preferences.sql` — junction table\n- `V14__create_user_wallets.sql` — user_wallets\n- `V15__create_refresh_tokens.sql` — refresh_tokens\n- `V16__create_password_reset_tokens.sql` — password_reset_tokens\n\n> Note: `preference_options` must be created before `user_settings` due to the FK on `preferred_city_option_id`.\n\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 27
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:12:43.940Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:12:43.940Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_1T24pbge4FgdvhQGW87CGimv",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6b3b77\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2299\nOutput:\n\n---\n\n## 7. API Contracts\n\n### Public Auth Endpoints\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/register`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string, \"password\": string }`\n  - Role is assigned server-side as `USER`. No role field accepted. Unknown fields rejected with `400`.\n  - Response: `RegisterResponse` → `{ \"userId\": Long }`\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/login`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string, \"password\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"accessToken\": string (JWT, 1h), \"refreshToken\": string (opaque random string, 1d) }`\n  - Note: `accessToken` is a signed JWT. `refreshToken` is an opaque random string stored as hash in DB — it is NOT a JWT.\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/token/refresh`\n  - Request: `{ \"refreshToken\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"accessToken\": string }` (new JWT, 1h)\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`\n  - Request: `{ \"refreshToken\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Logged out successfully\" }`\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Password reset token generated\" }` (token not returned to client)\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm`\n  - Request: `token`, `newPassword`, `confirmPassword`\n  - Response: success\n\n### JWT-Protected Endpoints\n- `GET /api/v1/auth/me`\n  - Response: `UserProfileResponse` → `{ \"userId\": Long, \"email\": string, \"role\": string, \"status\": string }`\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/change`\n  - Request: `{ \"currentPassword\": string, \"newPassword\": string, \"confirmPassword\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Password changed successfully\" }`\n- `GET /api/v1/preferences/options`\n  - Response: `{ \"options\": [ { \"type\": \"CITY\"|\"GENRE\", \"value\": string } ] }`\n- `GET /api/v1/users/me/settings`\n  - Response: `{ \"fullName\": string, \"phone\": string, \"dob\": string, \"address\": string, \"preferredCity\": { \"id\": Long, \"value\": string }, \"preferredGenres\": [ { \"id\": Long, \"value\": string } ], \"notificationOptIn\": boolean }`\n- `PUT /api/v1/users/me/settings`\n  - Request: `{ \"fullName\": string, \"phone\": string, \"dob\": string, \"address\": string, \"preferredCityOptionId\": Long, \"preferredGenreOptionIds\": [Long, max 3], \"notificationOptIn\": boolean }`\n  - Client sends `preference_options.id` values (not free strings); server validates they exist and are active.\n  - Field names are camelCase in API; stored as FK references in DB.\n- `GET /api/v1/users/me/wallet`\n  - Response: `{ \"balance\": number, \"currency\": string }`\n\n### Error Response Shape (all endpoints)\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": \"DUPLICATE_EMAIL\",\n    \"message\": \"Email already in use\",\n    \"field\": \"email\"\n  }\n}\n```\n`field` is optional and only present for validation errors tied to a specific input field.\n\nError responses use `ErrorResponse` from `shared/common/dto`. Exception classes: `ResourceNotFoundException` (404), `BusinessRuleException` (4xx), `IntegrationException` (502 when applicable).\n\n---\n\n## 8. Business Logic\n\n### Validation Rules\n- Email uniqueness (case-insensitive) is checked **first**, before any other registration validation.\n- Role is never accepted from the client; server always sets `USER`.\n- Access token TTL = 1 hour (`SecurityConstants.ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_S`); refresh token TTL = 1 day (`SecurityConstants.REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_S`).\n- Password reset token TTL = **15 minutes** (`SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S`).\n- Logout revokes provided refresh token.\n- Password reset token must be latest, unexpired, and unconsumed.\n- Exactly 1 city and up to 3 unique genres.\n- Preferences must map to active options.\n\n### Security Rules\n- JWT-only security for non-auth endpoints. No Basic auth.\n- Users can only access their own settings/wallet.\n- Logout revokes the **provided refresh token only** (current session). A future `/logout-all` endpoint can extend to all sessions.\n\n### JWT Claim Contract\nApplies to **access tokens only**. Refresh tokens are opaque random strings (not JWTs); they carry no claims and are validated only by DB hash lookup + expiry + revocation check.\n\n| Claim | Value |\n|---|---|\n| `sub` | user `id` as String (BIGSERIAL Long — not UUID, consistent with all schema PKs) |\n| `role` | `USER` |\n| `iat` | issued-at epoch seconds |\n| `exp` | iat + 3600 (1 hour) |\n| `jti` | unique token UUID (for future revocation) |\n| `typ` | `access` (access tokens only; refresh tokens are not JWTs) |\n\n### External System Calls\n- None.\n\n### Concurrency & Integrity\n- Unique constraints enforce identity invariants (`email`, `user_id` 1:1 tables).\n\n---\n\n## 9. Error Handling\n\nAll exceptions are handled by `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/`. No module-level `@ControllerAdvice`.\n\n> Error response shape is defined in Section 7. All error responses use that shape: `{ \"error\": { \"code\": string, \"message\": string, \"field\"?: string } }`.\n\n| Scenario | Exception Class | HTTP | User Message |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Duplicate email on register | BusinessRuleException | 409 | \"Email already in use\" |\n| Invalid credentials | BusinessRuleException | 401 | \"Invalid credentials\" |\n| Missing/invalid JWT | — (Spring Security `AuthenticationEntryPoint`) | 401 | \"Unauthorized\" |\n| Forbidden operation | BusinessRuleException | 403 | \"Forbidden\" |\n| Refresh token invalid/expired/revoked | BusinessRuleException | 401 | \"Refresh token invalid\" |\n| Reset token invalid/expired/consumed/not-latest | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Reset token invalid\" |\n| Current password incorrect | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Current password incorrect\" |\n| Password mismatch | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Password mismatch\" |\n| Preference option inactive/not found | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Invalid preference\" |\n| Wallet not found | ResourceNotFoundException | 404 | \"Wallet not found\" |\n\n---\n\n## 10. Acceptance Criteria (Tests)\n\n### Layer 1 — `domain/` (Pure Unit Tests)\n- [ ] should_create_user_with_user_role_assigned_server_side\n- [ ] should_reject_duplicate_email_case_insensitive\n- [ ] should_enforce_city_option_required_and_max_three_unique_genre_options\n- [ ] should_reject_more_than_three_genre_preferences\n- [ ] should_reject_duplicate_genre_option_ids_in_same_settings\n- [ ] should_reject_expired_or_revoked_refresh_token\n- [ ] should_accept_only_latest_unconsumed_unexpired_reset_token\n- [ ] should_invalidate_all_prior_reset_tokens_when_new_token_issued\n- [ ] should_initialize_wallet_with_zero_balance_on_user_creation\n- [ ] should_reject_password_change_when_new_and_confirm_do_not_match\n\n### Layer 2 — `service/` (Mockito Unit Tests)\n- [ ] should_hash_password_with_bcrypt_and_save_user_on_register\n- [ ] should_create_wallet_for_user_on_registration\n- [ ] should_issue_access_and_refresh_tokens_on_login\n- [ ] should_revoke_refresh_token_on_logout\n- [ ] should_issue_new_access_token_when_refresh_token_valid\n- [ ] should_generate_reset_token_and_invalidate_previous_tokens\n- [ ] should_reset_password_when_token_is_latest_unexpired_unconsumed\n- [ ] should_change_password_when_current_password_matches\n- [ ] should_validate_active_preference_options_before_upsert\n\n### Layer 3 — `api/` (@WebMvcTest)\n- [ ] should_return_201_on_register_when_payload_valid\n- [ ] should_return_409_on_register_when_email_exists\n- [ ] should_return_400_on_register_when_role_field_included_in_request\n- [ ] should_return_200_and_tokens_on_login_when_credentials_valid\n- [ ] should_return_401_on_login_when_credentials_invalid\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_token_refresh_when_refresh_token_valid\n- [ ] should_return_401_on_token_refresh_when_refresh_token_invalid\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_logout\n- [ ] should_return_401_for_protected_endpoint_when_jwt_missing_or_expired\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_settings_upsert_when_preferences_valid\n- [ ] should_return_400_on_settings_upsert_when_city_missing_or_genres_exceed_limit\n\n### Layer 4 — `arch/` (ArchUnit)\n- [ ] should_enforce_identity_module_structure_and_layer_boundaries\n- [ ] should_prevent_identity_from_direct_external_http_calls\n- [ ] should_enforce_shared_global_exception_handler_only\n\n---\n\n## 11. Finalized Decisions\n\nAll decisions are closed. Values are enforced in Sections 3, 7, 8, and 9.\n\n| Decision | Value | Location |\n|---|---|---|\n| Password reset token TTL | 15 minutes | Section 8 — Validation Rules |\n| Logout scope | Revoke provided refresh token only | Section 8 — Security Rules |\n| JWT claim set | `sub` (user BIGSERIAL id as String), `role`, `iat`, `exp`, `jti`, `typ=access` — access tokens only | Section 8 — JWT Claim Contract |\n| Refresh token type | Opaque random string (not JWT); stored as hash in DB; validated by hash + expiry + revocation | Section 7, 8 |\n| Role in registration API | Removed; server default = `USER` | Section 3, 7 |\n| Duplicate email check order | First validation before all others | Section 8 — Validation Rules |\n| Preferred genres storage | `user_genre_preferences` junction table (FK to `preference_options`) | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| Preferred city storage | `preferred_city_option_id FK → preference_options(id)` | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| `preference_options.id` type | `BIGSERIAL` (consistent with all schema PKs) | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| `organization_id` on users | Omitted — booking users have no org context in this flow | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_1T24pbge4FgdvhQGW87CGimv",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6b3b77\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2299\nOutput:\n\n---\n\n## 7. API Contracts\n\n### Public Auth Endpoints\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/register`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string, \"password\": string }`\n  - Role is assigned server-side as `USER`. No role field accepted. Unknown fields rejected with `400`.\n  - Response: `RegisterResponse` → `{ \"userId\": Long }`\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/login`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string, \"password\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"accessToken\": string (JWT, 1h), \"refreshToken\": string (opaque random string, 1d) }`\n  - Note: `accessToken` is a signed JWT. `refreshToken` is an opaque random string stored as hash in DB — it is NOT a JWT.\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/token/refresh`\n  - Request: `{ \"refreshToken\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"accessToken\": string }` (new JWT, 1h)\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`\n  - Request: `{ \"refreshToken\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Logged out successfully\" }`\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset`\n  - Request: `{ \"email\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Password reset token generated\" }` (token not returned to client)\n\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm`\n  - Request: `token`, `newPassword`, `confirmPassword`\n  - Response: success\n\n### JWT-Protected Endpoints\n- `GET /api/v1/auth/me`\n  - Response: `UserProfileResponse` → `{ \"userId\": Long, \"email\": string, \"role\": string, \"status\": string }`\n- `POST /api/v1/auth/password/change`\n  - Request: `{ \"currentPassword\": string, \"newPassword\": string, \"confirmPassword\": string }`\n  - Response: `{ \"message\": \"Password changed successfully\" }`\n- `GET /api/v1/preferences/options`\n  - Response: `{ \"options\": [ { \"type\": \"CITY\"|\"GENRE\", \"value\": string } ] }`\n- `GET /api/v1/users/me/settings`\n  - Response: `{ \"fullName\": string, \"phone\": string, \"dob\": string, \"address\": string, \"preferredCity\": { \"id\": Long, \"value\": string }, \"preferredGenres\": [ { \"id\": Long, \"value\": string } ], \"notificationOptIn\": boolean }`\n- `PUT /api/v1/users/me/settings`\n  - Request: `{ \"fullName\": string, \"phone\": string, \"dob\": string, \"address\": string, \"preferredCityOptionId\": Long, \"preferredGenreOptionIds\": [Long, max 3], \"notificationOptIn\": boolean }`\n  - Client sends `preference_options.id` values (not free strings); server validates they exist and are active.\n  - Field names are camelCase in API; stored as FK references in DB.\n- `GET /api/v1/users/me/wallet`\n  - Response: `{ \"balance\": number, \"currency\": string }`\n\n### Error Response Shape (all endpoints)\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": \"DUPLICATE_EMAIL\",\n    \"message\": \"Email already in use\",\n    \"field\": \"email\"\n  }\n}\n```\n`field` is optional and only present for validation errors tied to a specific input field.\n\nError responses use `ErrorResponse` from `shared/common/dto`. Exception classes: `ResourceNotFoundException` (404), `BusinessRuleException` (4xx), `IntegrationException` (502 when applicable).\n\n---\n\n## 8. Business Logic\n\n### Validation Rules\n- Email uniqueness (case-insensitive) is checked **first**, before any other registration validation.\n- Role is never accepted from the client; server always sets `USER`.\n- Access token TTL = 1 hour (`SecurityConstants.ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_S`); refresh token TTL = 1 day (`SecurityConstants.REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_S`).\n- Password reset token TTL = **15 minutes** (`SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S`).\n- Logout revokes provided refresh token.\n- Password reset token must be latest, unexpired, and unconsumed.\n- Exactly 1 city and up to 3 unique genres.\n- Preferences must map to active options.\n\n### Security Rules\n- JWT-only security for non-auth endpoints. No Basic auth.\n- Users can only access their own settings/wallet.\n- Logout revokes the **provided refresh token only** (current session). A future `/logout-all` endpoint can extend to all sessions.\n\n### JWT Claim Contract\nApplies to **access tokens only**. Refresh tokens are opaque random strings (not JWTs); they carry no claims and are validated only by DB hash lookup + expiry + revocation check.\n\n| Claim | Value |\n|---|---|\n| `sub` | user `id` as String (BIGSERIAL Long — not UUID, consistent with all schema PKs) |\n| `role` | `USER` |\n| `iat` | issued-at epoch seconds |\n| `exp` | iat + 3600 (1 hour) |\n| `jti` | unique token UUID (for future revocation) |\n| `typ` | `access` (access tokens only; refresh tokens are not JWTs) |\n\n### External System Calls\n- None.\n\n### Concurrency & Integrity\n- Unique constraints enforce identity invariants (`email`, `user_id` 1:1 tables).\n\n---\n\n## 9. Error Handling\n\nAll exceptions are handled by `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/`. No module-level `@ControllerAdvice`.\n\n> Error response shape is defined in Section 7. All error responses use that shape: `{ \"error\": { \"code\": string, \"message\": string, \"field\"?: string } }`.\n\n| Scenario | Exception Class | HTTP | User Message |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Duplicate email on register | BusinessRuleException | 409 | \"Email already in use\" |\n| Invalid credentials | BusinessRuleException | 401 | \"Invalid credentials\" |\n| Missing/invalid JWT | — (Spring Security `AuthenticationEntryPoint`) | 401 | \"Unauthorized\" |\n| Forbidden operation | BusinessRuleException | 403 | \"Forbidden\" |\n| Refresh token invalid/expired/revoked | BusinessRuleException | 401 | \"Refresh token invalid\" |\n| Reset token invalid/expired/consumed/not-latest | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Reset token invalid\" |\n| Current password incorrect | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Current password incorrect\" |\n| Password mismatch | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Password mismatch\" |\n| Preference option inactive/not found | BusinessRuleException | 400 | \"Invalid preference\" |\n| Wallet not found | ResourceNotFoundException | 404 | \"Wallet not found\" |\n\n---\n\n## 10. Acceptance Criteria (Tests)\n\n### Layer 1 — `domain/` (Pure Unit Tests)\n- [ ] should_create_user_with_user_role_assigned_server_side\n- [ ] should_reject_duplicate_email_case_insensitive\n- [ ] should_enforce_city_option_required_and_max_three_unique_genre_options\n- [ ] should_reject_more_than_three_genre_preferences\n- [ ] should_reject_duplicate_genre_option_ids_in_same_settings\n- [ ] should_reject_expired_or_revoked_refresh_token\n- [ ] should_accept_only_latest_unconsumed_unexpired_reset_token\n- [ ] should_invalidate_all_prior_reset_tokens_when_new_token_issued\n- [ ] should_initialize_wallet_with_zero_balance_on_user_creation\n- [ ] should_reject_password_change_when_new_and_confirm_do_not_match\n\n### Layer 2 — `service/` (Mockito Unit Tests)\n- [ ] should_hash_password_with_bcrypt_and_save_user_on_register\n- [ ] should_create_wallet_for_user_on_registration\n- [ ] should_issue_access_and_refresh_tokens_on_login\n- [ ] should_revoke_refresh_token_on_logout\n- [ ] should_issue_new_access_token_when_refresh_token_valid\n- [ ] should_generate_reset_token_and_invalidate_previous_tokens\n- [ ] should_reset_password_when_token_is_latest_unexpired_unconsumed\n- [ ] should_change_password_when_current_password_matches\n- [ ] should_validate_active_preference_options_before_upsert\n\n### Layer 3 — `api/` (@WebMvcTest)\n- [ ] should_return_201_on_register_when_payload_valid\n- [ ] should_return_409_on_register_when_email_exists\n- [ ] should_return_400_on_register_when_role_field_included_in_request\n- [ ] should_return_200_and_tokens_on_login_when_credentials_valid\n- [ ] should_return_401_on_login_when_credentials_invalid\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_token_refresh_when_refresh_token_valid\n- [ ] should_return_401_on_token_refresh_when_refresh_token_invalid\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_logout\n- [ ] should_return_401_for_protected_endpoint_when_jwt_missing_or_expired\n- [ ] should_return_200_on_settings_upsert_when_preferences_valid\n- [ ] should_return_400_on_settings_upsert_when_city_missing_or_genres_exceed_limit\n\n### Layer 4 — `arch/` (ArchUnit)\n- [ ] should_enforce_identity_module_structure_and_layer_boundaries\n- [ ] should_prevent_identity_from_direct_external_http_calls\n- [ ] should_enforce_shared_global_exception_handler_only\n\n---\n\n## 11. Finalized Decisions\n\nAll decisions are closed. Values are enforced in Sections 3, 7, 8, and 9.\n\n| Decision | Value | Location |\n|---|---|---|\n| Password reset token TTL | 15 minutes | Section 8 — Validation Rules |\n| Logout scope | Revoke provided refresh token only | Section 8 — Security Rules |\n| JWT claim set | `sub` (user BIGSERIAL id as String), `role`, `iat`, `exp`, `jti`, `typ=access` — access tokens only | Section 8 — JWT Claim Contract |\n| Refresh token type | Opaque random string (not JWT); stored as hash in DB; validated by hash + expiry + revocation | Section 7, 8 |\n| Role in registration API | Removed; server default = `USER` | Section 3, 7 |\n| Duplicate email check order | First validation before all others | Section 8 — Validation Rules |\n| Preferred genres storage | `user_genre_preferences` junction table (FK to `preference_options`) | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| Preferred city storage | `preferred_city_option_id FK → preference_options(id)` | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| `preference_options.id` type | `BIGSERIAL` (consistent with all schema PKs) | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n| `organization_id` on users | Omitted — booking users have no org context in this flow | Section 6 — DB Schema |\n"
  }
}

## Entry 32
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:12:46.673Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:12:46.673Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_cSICpjnilm7HV0QNNnlBaoJT",
    "output": "Chunk ID: c15790\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3259\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. ## Entry 73
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:13:14.322Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:13:14.322Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_H8CLr1oUQLaNJ7tpAvaObYuq",
    "output": "Chunk ID: da16e0\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1113\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.shared.security;\n\nimport io.jsonwebtoken.Claims;\nimport io.jsonwebtoken.JwtException;\nimport io.jsonwebtoken.Jwts;\nimport jakarta.annotation.PostConstruct;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.security.KeyFactory;\nimport java.security.PrivateKey;\nimport java.security.PublicKey;\nimport java.security.spec.PKCS8EncodedKeySpec;\nimport java.security.spec.X509EncodedKeySpec;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.Base64;\nimport java.util.Date;\nimport java.util.UUID;\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;\nimport org.springframework.core.io.Resource;\nimport org.springframework.core.io.ResourceLoader;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\n\n@Component\npublic class JwtTokenProvider {\n\n    private final ResourceLoader resourceLoader;\n    private final String privateKeyPath;\n    private final String publicKeyPath;\n\n    private PrivateKey privateKey;\n    private PublicKey publicKey;\n\n    public JwtTokenProvider(\n        ResourceLoader resourceLoader,\n        @Value(\"${jwt.private-key-path}\") String privateKeyPath,\n        @Value(\"${jwt.public-key-path}\") String publicKeyPath\n    ) {\n        this.resourceLoader = resourceLoader;\n        this.privateKeyPath = privateKeyPath;\n        this.publicKeyPath = publicKeyPath;\n    }\n\n    @PostConstruct\n    void init() {\n        this.privateKey = loadPrivateKey(privateKeyPath);\n        this.publicKey = loadPublicKey(publicKeyPath);\n    }\n\n    public String generateToken(Long userId, String role) {\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        return Jwts.builder()\n            .subject(String.valueOf(userId))\n            .claim(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_ROLE, role)\n            .claim(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_TYPE, SecurityConstants.TOKEN_TYPE_ACCESS)\n            .id(UUID.randomUUID().toString())\n            .issuedAt(Date.from(now))\n            .expiration(Date.from(now.plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_S)))\n            .signWith(privateKey, Jwts.SIG.RS256)\n            .compact();\n    }\n\n    public boolean validateToken(String token) {\n        if (token == null || token.isBlank()) {\n            return false;\n        }\n        try {\n            Jwts.parser().verifyWith(publicKey).build().parseSignedClaims(token);\n            return true;\n        } catch (JwtException | IllegalArgumentException ex) {\n            return false;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public Long extractUserId(String token) {\n        Claims claims = parseClaims(token);\n        return Long.parseLong(claims.getSubject());\n    }\n\n    public String extractRole(String token) {\n        Claims claims = parseClaims(token);\n        return claims.get(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_ROLE, String.class);\n    }\n\n    private Claims parseClaims(String token) {\n        return Jwts.parser().verifyWith(publicKey).build().parseSignedClaims(token).getPayload();\n    }\n\n    private PrivateKey loadPrivateKey(String location) {\n        byte[] keyBytes = readPemContent(location, \"PRIVATE KEY\");\n        PKCS8EncodedKeySpec spec = new PKCS8EncodedKeySpec(keyBytes);\n        try {\n            return KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePrivate(spec);\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Failed to load RSA private key\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private PublicKey loadPublicKey(String location) {\n        byte[] keyBytes = readPemContent(location, \"PUBLIC KEY\");\n        X509EncodedKeySpec spec = new X509EncodedKeySpec(keyBytes);\n        try {\n            return KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePublic(spec);\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Failed to load RSA public key\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private byte[] readPemContent(String location, String keyType) {\n        Resource resource = resourceLoader.getResource(location);\n        try (InputStream inputStream = resource.getInputStream()) {\n            String pem = new String(inputStream.readAllBytes(), StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n            String sanitized = pem\n                .replace(\"-----BEGIN \" + org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\n\n@Component\npublic class JwtTokenProvider {\n\n    private final ResourceLoader resourceLoader;\n    private final String privateKeyPath;\n    private this.publicKey = loadPublicKey(publicKeyPath);\n    }\n\n    public String generateToken(Long userId, String role) {\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        return Jwts.builder()\n            .subject(String.valueOf(userId))\n            .claim(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_ROLE, role)\n            .claim(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_TYPE, SecurityConstants.TOKEN_TYPE_ACCESS)\n extractRole(String token) {\n        Claims claims = parseClaims(token);\n        return claims.get(SecurityConstants.TOKEN_CLAIM_ROLE, String.class);\n    }\n\n    private Claims parseClaims(String KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePrivate(spec);\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Failed to load RSA private key\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n try {\n            return KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePublic(spec);\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Failed to load RSA public key\", ex);\n keyType + \"-----\", \"\")\n                .replace(\"-----END \" + keyType + \"-----\", \"\")\n                .replaceAll(\"\\\\s\", \"\");\n            return ebEventSyncService.createDraft(organizationId, toEbCreateRequest(slot));\\\\n            slot.setEbEventId(created.id());\\\\n            slot.recordSyncFailure(null);\\\\n            showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(\\\\n                slot.getId(),\\\\n                slot.getEbEventId(),\\\\n                slot.getPricingTiers().stream().map(ShowSlotPricingTier::getId).toList(),\\\\n                slot.getSeatingMode(),\\\\n                organizationId,\\\\n @Transactional\\\\n    public void onTicketSyncComplete(TicketSyncCompletedEvent event, Long organizationId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(event.slotId());\\\\n        try {\\\\n            ebEventSyncService.publishEvent(organizationId, event.ebEventId());\\\\n            stateMachineService.sendEvent(slot, ShowSlotEvent.EB_PUBLISHED);\\\\n            showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new ShowSlotActivatedEvent(\\\\n                slot.getId(),\\\\n                slot.getEbEventId(),\\\\n                organizationId,\\\\n                slot.getVenueId(),\\\\n                slot.getCityId()\\\\n            ));\\\\n            if (slot.isRecurring() && slot.getRecurrenceRule() != null) {\\\\n child.setEbEventId(occurrence.eventId());\\\\n                        slot.addOccurrence(child);\\\\n                    }\\\\n                }\\\\n                showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n            }\\\\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\\\\n            slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\\\\n            showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n            eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotSyncFailedEvent(slot.getId(), ex.getMessage()));\\\\n            throw ex;\\\\n        }\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public void onTicketSyncFailed(TicketSyncFailedEvent event) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(event.slotId());\\\\n        slot.recordSyncFailure(event.reason());\\\\n        showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotSyncFailedEvent(slot.getId(), event.reason()));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public ShowSlot updateSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId, UpdateShowSlotRequest request) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Cannot update a cancelled slot\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_CANCELLED\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        Integer previousCapacity = slot.getCapacity();\\\\n        slot.updateDetails(request.title(), request.description(), request.startTime(), request.endTime(), request.capacity());\\\\n\\\\n        if (request.pricingTiers() != null) {\\\\n            slot.clearPricingTiers();\\\\n            showSlotMapper.toPricingTiers(request.pricingTiers()).forEach(slot::addPricingTier);\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        slot = showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE) {\\\\n            try {\\\\n                ebEventSyncService.updateEvent(organizationId, slot.getEbEventId(), toEbUpdateRequest(slot));\\\\n                if (request.capacity() != null && !request.capacity().equals(previousCapacity)) {\\\\n                    ebCapacityService.updateCapacityTier(slot.getEbEventId(), request.capacity());\\\\n                }\\\\n            } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\\\\n                slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\\\\n                showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n                throw ex;\\\\n            }\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        return slot;\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public boolean cancelSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot already cancelled\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_ALREADY_CANCELLED\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        stateMachineService.sendEvent(slot, ShowSlotEvent.CANCEL);\\\\n        showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n\\\\n        boolean ebCancelled = true;\\\\n        try {\\\\n            if (slot.getEbEventId() != null) {\\\\n                ebEventSyncService.cancelEvent(organizationId, slot.getEbEventId());\\\\n            }\\\\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\\\\n            ebCancelled = false;\\\\n            slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\\\\n            showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n        }\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new ShowSlotCancelledEvent(slot.getId(), slot.getEbEventId(), organizationId, slot.getVenueId(), slot.getCityId()));\\\\n        return ebCancelled;\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public void retrySync(Long organizationId, Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() != ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot is not pending sync\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_NOT_PENDING\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (slot.getEbEventId() == null) {\\\\n            submitSlot(organizationId, slotId);\\\\n            return;\\\\n        }\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(\\\\n            slot.getId(),\\\\n            slot.getEbEventId(),\\\\n            slot.getPricingTiers().stream().map(ShowSlotPricingTier::getId).toList(),\\\\n            slot.getSeatingMode(),\\\\n            organizationId,\\\\n            slot.getSourceSeatMapId()\\\\n        ));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public ShowSlot getSlot(Long slotId) {\\\\n        return getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public List<ShowSlotOccurrence> listOccurrences(Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (!slot.isRecurring()) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot is not recurring\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_NOT_RECURRING\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        return occurrenceRepository.findByParentSlotIdOrderByOccurrenceIndexAsc(slotId);\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public Page<ShowSlot> listSlots(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Long venueId, Long cityId,\\\\n                                    Instant startAfter, int page, int size) {\\\\n        Specification<ShowSlot> spec = (root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"organizationId\\\\\\\"), organizationId);\\\\n        if (status != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\"), status));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (venueId != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"venueId\\\\\\\"), venueId));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (cityId != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"cityId\\\\\\\"), cityId));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (startAfter != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.greaterThan(root.get(\\\\\\\"startTime\\\\\\\"), startAfter));\\\\n        }\\\\n        return showSlotRepository.findAll(spec, PageRequest.of(page, size));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public Page<ShowSlot> listMismatches(Long organizationId, int page, int size) {\\\\n        return showSlotRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndLastSyncErrorIsNotNull(\\\\n            organizationId,\\\\n            ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE,\\\\n            PageRequest.of(page, size)\\\\n
