(404)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist() throws Exception {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId))\\n        .thenThrow(new BookingNotFoundException(bookingId));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\\\"/api/bookings/{id}\\\", bookingId)\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isNotFound())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\n            containsString(\\\"Booking with id \\\" + bookingId + \\\" not found\\\")));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — Business Rule Violation (422)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_422_when_cancellation_window_has_passed() throws Exception {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    when(bookingService.cancelBooking(bookingId))\\n        .thenThrow(new BusinessRuleException(\\\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\\\"));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(delete(\\\"/api/bookings/{id}\\\", bookingId)\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isUnprocessableEntity())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\n            containsString(\\\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours\\\")));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — External System Failure (502)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_502_when_payment_gateway_is_unavailable() throws Exception {\\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any()))\\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\\\"Payment gateway unavailable\\\"));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(validRequestBody())\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isBadGateway())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\\"A payment error occurred. Please try again.\\\"));\\n    // Verify: internal error message is NOT exposed to the client\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Authorization Tests\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_401_when_request_is_unauthenticated() throws Exception {\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(validRequestBody()))\\n        // No Authorization header\\n        .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized());\\n}\\n\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_403_when_user_lacks_admin_role() throws Exception {\\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\\\"/api/admin/bookings\\\")\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer user-token\\\")) // ROLE_USER, not ROLE_ADMIN\\n        .andExpect(status().isForbidden());\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Layer 4 — `arch/` Tests\\n\\n### Purpose\\n\\nEnforce architectural boundaries at build time using ArchUnit. A failing arch test means\\nsomeone violated a boundary — **fix the code, never weaken the rule.**\\n\\nEvery module must have exactly one `[Module]ArchTest.java` in its `arch/` test folder.\\nThese run as part of the normal test suite — no special setup or CI configuration needed.\\n\\n### Mandatory Rules (Copy for Every Module)\\n\\n```java\\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \\\"com.[org].[module]\\\")\\nclass [Module]ArchTest {\\n\\n    // Rule 1: Domain must be pure — no infrastructure dependencies\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule domain_must_not_depend_on_infrastructure =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].domain..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[module].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"org.springframework.web..\\\",\\n                \\\"jakarta.persistence..\\\",\\n                \\\"org.springframework.data..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Domain must be pure Java — no JPA, no HTTP, no Spring annotations\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 2: Controllers must not bypass the service layer\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule controllers_must_not_import_repositories =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].api.controller..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].repository..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Controllers must delegate to service — never access repositories directly\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 3: No module imports another module's repository\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_repository_access =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].repository..\\\"\\n                // add all peer and downstream modules here\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Modules must never import another module's repository — call their API instead\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 4: Events must carry only primitives and value objects — never JPA entities\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule events_must_not_contain_entities =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].event.published..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().areAnnotatedWith(Entity.class)\\n            .because(\\\"Events must carry only primitives, IDs, enums, or value objects — never JPA entities\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 5: Mappers must not call services — pure translation only\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule mappers_must_not_call_services =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].mapper..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].service..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Mappers are pure translation — they must not call services or trigger business logic\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 6: No direct external system calls — must use shared/ ACL facades\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_eventbrite_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.eventbrite.client..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Eventbrite calls must use EbEventSyncService, EbTicketService, etc. from shared/eventbrite/service/\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_razorpay_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.razorpay..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Razorpay calls must use RazorpayAclService from shared/payment/service/\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_openai_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.openai..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"OpenAI calls must use ChatbotAclService from shared/ai/service/\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 7: No module imports another module's service, entity, or repository\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_service_imports =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].service..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].service..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].domain..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].domain..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].repository..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Modules communicate only via REST API or Spring Events — never import another module's internal classes\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 8: shared/ can NEVER depend on any module\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule shared_must_not_depend_on_modules =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..shared..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..discovery-catalog..\\\",\\n                \\\"..scheduling..\\\",\\n                \\\"..identity..\\\",\\n                \\\"..booking-inventory..\\\",\\n                \\\"..payments-ticketing..\\\",\\n                \\\"..promotions..\\\",\\n                \\\"..engagement..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"shared/ is foundational — it cannot depend on any module\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 9: Only one GlobalExceptionHandler exists in shared/common/exception/\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule only_one_global_exception_handler =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].api.exception..\\\")\\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class)\\n            .because(\\\"All exception handling routes through shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler — never add @ControllerAdvice in a module\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 10: Dependency direction is strictly downward; no circular imports\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule module_dependency_direction_is_downward =\\n        layeredArchitecture()\\n            .layer(\\\"shared\\\").definedBy(\\\"..shared..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"discovery-catalog\\\").definedBy(\\\"..discovery-catalog..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"scheduling\\\").definedBy(\\\"..scheduling..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"identity\\\").definedBy(\\\"..identity..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"booking-inventory\\\").definedBy(\\\"..booking-inventory..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\").definedBy(\\\"..payments-ticketing..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"promotions\\\").definedBy(\\\"..promotions..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"engagement\\\").definedBy(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"admin\\\").definedBy(\\\"..admin..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"app\\\").definedBy(\\\"..app..\\\")\\n            \\n            .whereLayer(\\\"app\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"admin\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"engagement\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"promotions\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"booking-inventory\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"scheduling\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"identity\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"discovery-catalog\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"scheduling\\\", \\\"identity\\\", \\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"shared\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            \\n            .because(\\\"Dependency order is: shared ← discovery-catalog ← scheduling/identity ← booking-inventory ← payments-ticketing ← promotions ← engagement ← admin ← app. No circular dependencies allowed.\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Testing Eventbrite Integration Rules\\n\\nAdd these additional rules to any module that integrates with Eventbrite to enforce ACL facade usage:\\n\\n```java\\n    // Rule: Only EbEventSyncService may read Eventbrite events\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbEventSyncService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbVenueService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbScheduleService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbTicketService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbCapacityService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbOrderService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbAttendeeService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbDiscountSyncService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbRefundService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbWebhookService\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..eventbrite..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Only the 10 ACL facades in shared/eventbrite/service/ may call Eventbrite API\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule: User and Order creation must never happen via Eventbrite API\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_eventbrite_user_or_order_creation =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].service..\\\")\\n            .should().callMethodWhere(\\n                target -> target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbAttendeeService.createUser\\\") ||\\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbOrderService.createOrder\\\") ||\\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbOrderService.create\\\")\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"User registration and order creation are internal-only — Eventbrite has no API for these; \\\" +\\n                     \\\"use identity module for users and booking module for orders; JavaScript SDK handles Eventbrite order creation\\\");\\n```\\n\\n### When to Add a New Arch Rule\\n\\nAdd a new rule to `[Module]ArchTest.java` whenever:\\n- A new architectural decision is made for this module\\n- A new layer or package is introduced\\n- A new external system integration is added\\n- A code review reveals a violation that should be prevented automatically in future\\n\\nClear example: If you implement a seat-locking state machine, add a rule preventing any @Service from bypassing the state machine and directly updating seat status.\\n\\nNever add a rule to suppress a valid violation. Fix the code.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Mapper Tests\\n\\n### Purpose\\n\\nVerify that every field is correctly translated in both directions: domain → response DTO,\\nand request DTO → domain object. Mappers are the contract between your domain and your API —\\na wrong mapping silently breaks both.\\n\\n### Annotations\\n\\nNone. Instantiate the MapStruct implementation directly.\\n\\n```java\\nclass BookingMapperTest {\\n    // Instantiate directly — no Spring context needed\\n    private final BookingMapper mapper = Mappers.getMapper(BookingMapper.class);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Happy Path — Domain to Response\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_fields() {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\\n\\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\\n\\n    assertThat(response.getId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\\n    assertThat(response.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\\n    assertThat(response.getStatus()).isEqualTo(\\\"CONFIRMED\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Happy Path — Request DTO to Domain\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_create_request_to_booking_domain_with_correct_fields() {\\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    List<UUID> seatIds = List.of(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID());\\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\\n\\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\\n\\n    assertThat(booking.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\\n    assertThat(booking.getSeatIds()).containsExactlyElementsOf(seatIds);\\n    assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Edge Case — Null / Optional Fields\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_null_cancellation_reason_to_null_in_response() {\\n    Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\\n    booking.setCancellationReason(null);\\n\\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\\n\\n    assertThat(response.getCancellationReason()).isNull();\\n}\\n\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_optional_notes_field_when_present() {\\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\\n    request.setNotes(\\\"Wheelchair access required\\\");\\n\\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\\n\\n    assertThat(booking.getNotes()).isEqualTo(\\\"Wheelchair access required\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference — Which Test Goes Where?\\n\\nUse this table to decide the correct layer for any test scenario:\\n\\n| Scenario | Correct Layer | Reason |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `booking.confirm()` succeeds when status is PENDING | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\\n| `booking.confirm()` throws when status is CANCELLED | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` saves and publishes event | `service/` | Orchestration |\\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` throws when booking not found | `service/` | Orchestration — entity lookup |\\n| `BookingService.initiateBooking()` calls PaymentAclService | `service/` | Orchestration — ACL call |\\n| `BookingService` refunds when DB write fails after payment | `service/` | Compensation logic |\\n| `GET /api/bookings/{id}` returns 200 with correct JSON | `api/` | HTTP contract |\\n| `POST /api/bookings` returns 400 for missing seatIds | `api/` | Request validation |\\n| `DELETE /api/bookings/{id}` returns 403 without admin role | `api/` | Authorization |\\n| No class in `booking.domain` imports a `@Repository` | `arch/` | Boundary rule |\\n| No class in `booking` imports `payments.repository` | `arch/` | Cross-module boundary |\\n| Events in `booking.event.published` carry no `@Entity` | `arch/` | Event payload rule |\\n| `BookingMapper.toResponse()` maps all fields correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\\n| `BookingMapper.toDomain()` maps request to domain correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\\n\\n| Mistake | Why It's Wrong | Fix |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Testing `booking.confirm()` logic in `service/` tests | Re-tests domain rules — adds no value | Move to `domain/` |\\n| Using `@SpringBootTest` in `service/` or `domain/` tests | Loads entire context — massively slow | Use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` or no annotation |\\n| Putting `@MockBean` in `domain/` tests | If you need a mock, domain has an infra dependency | Remove the dependency from the domain class |\\n| Testing business logic in `api/` tests | Controller tests should only care about HTTP contract | Mock the service; move logic test to `service/` or `domain/` |\\n| Deleting or weakening an `arch/` rule to make a test pass | Hides a real architectural violation | Fix the production code, not the test |\\n| Using `assertThat(response).isNotNull()` as the only assertion | Proves nothing — a null check is not a contract | Assert every field that matters to the caller |\\n| Missing event payload assertions in `service/` tests | A wrong payload breaks the consuming module silently | Use `ArgumentCaptor` to assert every event field |\\n| Mapping fields inside `service/` or `controller/` tests | Indicates production code is mapping in the wrong place | All mapping belongs in `mapper/` — fix the source |\\n\"\n"
  }
}

onTicketSyncFailed(TicketSyncFailedEvent event) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(event.slotId());\\\\n        slot.recordSyncFailure(event.reason());\\\\n        showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotSyncFailedEvent(slot.getId(), event.reason()));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public ShowSlot updateSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId, UpdateShowSlotRequest request) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Cannot update a cancelled slot\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_CANCELLED\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        Integer previousCapacity = slot.getCapacity();\\\\n        slot.updateDetails(request.title(), request.description(), request.startTime(), request.endTime(), request.capacity());\\\\n\\\\n        if (request.pricingTiers() != null) {\\\\n            slot.clearPricingTiers();\\\\n            showSlotMapper.toPricingTiers(request.pricingTiers()).forEach(slot::addPricingTier);\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        slot = showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE) {\\\\n            try {\\\\n                ebEventSyncService.updateEvent(organizationId, slot.getEbEventId(), toEbUpdateRequest(slot));\\\\n                if (request.capacity() != null && !request.capacity().equals(previousCapacity)) {\\\\n                    ebCapacityService.updateCapacityTier(slot.getEbEventId(), request.capacity());\\\\n                }\\\\n            } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\\\\n                slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\\\\n                showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n                throw ex;\\\\n            }\\\\n        }\\\\n\\\\n        return slot;\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public boolean cancelSlot(Long organizationId, Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot already cancelled\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_ALREADY_CANCELLED\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        stateMachineService.sendEvent(slot, ShowSlotEvent.CANCEL);\\\\n        showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n\\\\n        boolean ebCancelled = true;\\\\n        try {\\\\n            if (slot.getEbEventId() != null) {\\\\n                ebEventSyncService.cancelEvent(organizationId, slot.getEbEventId());\\\\n            }\\\\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\\\\n            ebCancelled = false;\\\\n            slot.recordSyncFailure(ex.getMessage());\\\\n            showSlotRepository.save(slot);\\\\n        }\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new ShowSlotCancelledEvent(slot.getId(), slot.getEbEventId(), organizationId, slot.getVenueId(), slot.getCityId()));\\\\n        return ebCancelled;\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional\\\\n    public void retrySync(Long organizationId, Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (slot.getStatus() != ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot is not pending sync\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_NOT_PENDING\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (slot.getEbEventId() == null) {\\\\n            submitSlot(organizationId, slotId);\\\\n            return;\\\\n        }\\\\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new SlotDraftCreatedEvent(\\\\n            slot.getId(),\\\\n            slot.getEbEventId(),\\\\n            slot.getPricingTiers().stream().map(ShowSlotPricingTier::getId).toList(),\\\\n            slot.getSeatingMode(),\\\\n            organizationId,\\\\n            slot.getSourceSeatMapId()\\\\n        ));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public ShowSlot getSlot(Long slotId) {\\\\n        return getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public List<ShowSlotOccurrence> listOccurrences(Long slotId) {\\\\n        ShowSlot slot = getSlotOrThrow(slotId);\\\\n        if (!slot.isRecurring()) {\\\\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\\\\\\\"Slot is not recurring\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"SLOT_NOT_RECURRING\\\\\\\");\\\\n        }\\\\n        return occurrenceRepository.findByParentSlotIdOrderByOccurrenceIndexAsc(slotId);\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public Page<ShowSlot> listSlots(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Long venueId, Long cityId,\\\\n                                    Instant startAfter, int page, int size) {\\\\n        Specification<ShowSlot> spec = (root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"organizationId\\\\\\\"), organizationId);\\\\n        if (status != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\"), status));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (venueId != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"venueId\\\\\\\"), venueId));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (cityId != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.equal(root.get(\\\\\\\"cityId\\\\\\\"), cityId));\\\\n        }\\\\n        if (startAfter != null) {\\\\n            spec = spec.and((root, query, cb) -> cb.greaterThan(root.get(\\\\\\\"startTime\\\\\\\"), startAfter));\\\\n        }\\\\n        return showSlotRepository.findAll(spec, PageRequest.of(page, size));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\\\n    public Page<ShowSlot> listMismatches(Long organizationId, int page, int size) {\\\\n        return showSlotRepository.findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndLastSyncErrorIsNotNull(\\\\n            organizationId,\\\\n            ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE,\\\\n            PageRequest.of(page, size)\\\\n        );\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    private ShowSlot getSlotOrThrow(Long slotId) {\\\\n        return showSlotRepository.findById(slotId)\\\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new SchedulingNotFoundException(\\\\\\\"Slot not found: \\\\\\\" + slotId, \\\\\\\"SLOT_NOT_FOUND\\\\\\\"));\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    private EbEventCreateRequest toEbCreateRequest(ShowSlot slot) {\\\\n        return new EbEventCreateRequest(\\\\n            slot.getTitle(),\\\\n            slot.getDescription(),\\\\n            slot.getVenueId().toString(),\\\\n            slot.getStartTime(),\\\\n            slot.getEndTime(),\\\\n            slot.getCapacity(),\\\\n            slot.isRecurring(),\\\\n            null\\\\n        );\\\\n    }\\\\n\\\\n    private EbEventUpdateRequest toEbUpdateRequest(ShowSlot slot) {\\\\n        return new EbEventUpdateRequest(\\\\n            slot.getTitle(),\\\\n            slot.getDescription(),\\\\n            slot.getStartTime(),\\\\n            slot.getEndTime()\\\\n        );\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\nEOF\\\"}\",\nPOQ/Show_Venues_Events/Flow2_Venues_Events_Implementation.md:38425:    \"arguments\": \"{\\\"cmd\\\":\\\"cat > com.eventplatform.scheduling.service.client.CatalogVenueResponse;\\\\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.service.client.VenueCatalogClient;\\\\nimport com.eventplat…85075 tokens truncated…      .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isCreated())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.status\\\").value(\\\"PENDING\\\"));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — Validation (400)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_ids_is_missing() throws Exception {\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(\\\"{}\\\")   // missing required fields\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").exists());  // ErrorResponse shape\\n}\\n\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() throws Exception {\\n    // Build a request with 9 seats (max is 8)\\n    String body = buildRequestWithSeatCount(9);\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(body)\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — Not Found (404)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist() throws Exception {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    when(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId))\\n        .thenThrow(new BookingNotFoundException(bookingId));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\\\"/api/bookings/{id}\\\", bookingId)\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isNotFound())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\n            containsString(\\\"Booking with id \\\" + bookingId + \\\" not found\\\")));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — Business Rule Violation (422)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_422_when_cancellation_window_has_passed() throws Exception {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    when(bookingService.cancelBooking(bookingId))\\n        .thenThrow(new BusinessRuleException(\\\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\\\"));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(delete(\\\"/api/bookings/{id}\\\", bookingId)\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isUnprocessableEntity())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\n            containsString(\\\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours\\\")));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Negative Path Tests — External System Failure (502)\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_502_when_payment_gateway_is_unavailable() throws Exception {\\n    when(bookingService.initiateBooking(any()))\\n        .thenThrow(new PaymentIntegrationException(\\\"Payment gateway unavailable\\\"));\\n\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(validRequestBody())\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer test-token\\\"))\\n        .andExpect(status().isBadGateway())\\n        .andExpect(jsonPath(\\\"$.message\\\").value(\\\"A payment error occurred. Please try again.\\\"));\\n    // Verify: internal error message is NOT exposed to the client\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Authorization Tests\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_401_when_request_is_unauthenticated() throws Exception {\\n    mockMvc.perform(post(\\\"/api/bookings\\\")\\n            .contentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)\\n            .content(validRequestBody()))\\n        // No Authorization header\\n        .andExpect(status().isUnauthorized());\\n}\\n\\n@Test\\nvoid should_return_403_when_user_lacks_admin_role() throws Exception {\\n    mockMvc.perform(get(\\\"/api/admin/bookings\\\")\\n            .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", \\\"Bearer user-token\\\")) // ROLE_USER, not ROLE_ADMIN\\n        .andExpect(status().isForbidden());\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Layer 4 — `arch/` Tests\\n\\n### Purpose\\n\\nEnforce architectural boundaries at build time using ArchUnit. A failing arch test means\\nsomeone violated a boundary — **fix the code, never weaken the rule.**\\n\\nEvery module must have exactly one `[Module]ArchTest.java` in its `arch/` test folder.\\nThese run as part of the normal test suite — no special setup or CI configuration needed.\\n\\n### Mandatory Rules (Copy for Every Module)\\n\\n```java\\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \\\"com.[org].[module]\\\")\\nclass [Module]ArchTest {\\n\\n    // Rule 1: Domain must be pure — no infrastructure dependencies\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule domain_must_not_depend_on_infrastructure =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].domain..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[module].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"org.springframework.web..\\\",\\n                \\\"jakarta.persistence..\\\",\\n                \\\"org.springframework.data..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Domain must be pure Java — no JPA, no HTTP, no Spring annotations\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 2: Controllers must not bypass the service layer\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule controllers_must_not_import_repositories =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].api.controller..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].repository..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Controllers must delegate to service — never access repositories directly\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 3: No module imports another module's repository\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_repository_access =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].repository..\\\"\\n                // add all peer and downstream modules here\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Modules must never import another module's repository — call their API instead\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 4: Events must carry only primitives and value objects — never JPA entities\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule events_must_not_contain_entities =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].event.published..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().areAnnotatedWith(Entity.class)\\n            .because(\\\"Events must carry only primitives, IDs, enums, or value objects — never JPA entities\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 5: Mappers must not call services — pure translation only\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule mappers_must_not_call_services =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].mapper..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].service..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Mappers are pure translation — they must not call services or trigger business logic\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 6: No direct external system calls — must use shared/ ACL facades\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_eventbrite_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.eventbrite.client..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Eventbrite calls must use EbEventSyncService, EbTicketService, etc. from shared/eventbrite/service/\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_razorpay_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.razorpay..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Razorpay calls must use RazorpayAclService from shared/payment/service/\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_direct_openai_calls =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"com.openai..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"OpenAI calls must use ChatbotAclService from shared/ai/service/\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 7: No module imports another module's service, entity, or repository\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_cross_module_service_imports =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].service..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].service..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].domain..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].domain..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-a].repository..\\\",\\n                \\\"..[other-module-b].repository..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"Modules communicate only via REST API or Spring Events — never import another module's internal classes\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 8: shared/ can NEVER depend on any module\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule shared_must_not_depend_on_modules =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..shared..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..discovery-catalog..\\\",\\n                \\\"..scheduling..\\\",\\n                \\\"..identity..\\\",\\n                \\\"..booking-inventory..\\\",\\n                \\\"..payments-ticketing..\\\",\\n                \\\"..promotions..\\\",\\n                \\\"..engagement..\\\"\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"shared/ is foundational — it cannot depend on any module\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 9: Only one GlobalExceptionHandler exists in shared/common/exception/\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule only_one_global_exception_handler =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].api.exception..\\\")\\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class)\\n            .because(\\\"All exception handling routes through shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler — never add @ControllerAdvice in a module\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule 10: Dependency direction is strictly downward; no circular imports\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule module_dependency_direction_is_downward =\\n        layeredArchitecture()\\n            .layer(\\\"shared\\\").definedBy(\\\"..shared..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"discovery-catalog\\\").definedBy(\\\"..discovery-catalog..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"scheduling\\\").definedBy(\\\"..scheduling..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"identity\\\").definedBy(\\\"..identity..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"booking-inventory\\\").definedBy(\\\"..booking-inventory..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\").definedBy(\\\"..payments-ticketing..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"promotions\\\").definedBy(\\\"..promotions..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"engagement\\\").definedBy(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"admin\\\").definedBy(\\\"..admin..\\\")\\n            .layer(\\\"app\\\").definedBy(\\\"..app..\\\")\\n            \\n            .whereLayer(\\\"app\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"admin\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"engagement\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"promotions\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"booking-inventory\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"scheduling\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"identity\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"discovery-catalog\\\").mayNotAccessLayers(\\\"scheduling\\\", \\\"identity\\\", \\\"booking-inventory\\\", \\\"payments-ticketing\\\", \\\"promotions\\\", \\\"engagement\\\", \\\"admin\\\", \\\"app\\\")\\n            .whereLayer(\\\"shared\\\").mayNotAccessAnyLayer()\\n            \\n            .because(\\\"Dependency order is: shared ← discovery-catalog ← scheduling/identity ← booking-inventory ← payments-ticketing ← promotions ← engagement ← admin ← app. No circular dependencies allowed.\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Testing Eventbrite Integration Rules\\n\\nAdd these additional rules to any module that integrates with Eventbrite to enforce ACL facade usage:\\n\\n```java\\n    // Rule: Only EbEventSyncService may read Eventbrite events\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule only_acl_service_calls_eventbrite_api =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module]..\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbEventSyncService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbVenueService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbScheduleService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbTicketService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbCapacityService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbOrderService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbAttendeeService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbDiscountSyncService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbRefundService\\\")\\n            .and().doNotHaveSimpleName(\\\"EbWebhookService\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\\n            .resideInAPackage(\\\"..eventbrite..\\\")\\n            .because(\\\"Only the 10 ACL facades in shared/eventbrite/service/ may call Eventbrite API\\\");\\n\\n    // Rule: User and Order creation must never happen via Eventbrite API\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule no_eventbrite_user_or_order_creation =\\n        noClasses()\\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..[module].service..\\\")\\n            .should().callMethodWhere(\\n                target -> target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbAttendeeService.createUser\\\") ||\\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbOrderService.createOrder\\\") ||\\n                          target.getFullName().contains(\\\"EbOrderService.create\\\")\\n            )\\n            .because(\\\"User registration and order creation are internal-only — Eventbrite has no API for these; \\\" +\\n                     \\\"use identity module for users and booking module for orders; JavaScript SDK handles Eventbrite order creation\\\");\\n```\\n\\n### When to Add a New Arch Rule\\n\\nAdd a new rule to `[Module]ArchTest.java` whenever:\\n- A new architectural decision is made for this module\\n- A new layer or package is introduced\\n- A new external system integration is added\\n- A code review reveals a violation that should be prevented automatically in future\\n\\nClear example: If you implement a seat-locking state machine, add a rule preventing any @Service from bypassing the state machine and directly updating seat status.\\n\\nNever add a rule to suppress a valid violation. Fix the code.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Mapper Tests\\n\\n### Purpose\\n\\nVerify that every field is correctly translated in both directions: domain → response DTO,\\nand request DTO → domain object. Mappers are the contract between your domain and your API —\\na wrong mapping silently breaks both.\\n\\n### Annotations\\n\\nNone. Instantiate the MapStruct implementation directly.\\n\\n```java\\nclass BookingMapperTest {\\n    // Instantiate directly — no Spring context needed\\n    private final BookingMapper mapper = Mappers.getMapper(BookingMapper.class);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Happy Path — Domain to Response\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_fields() {\\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, userId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\\n\\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\\n\\n    assertThat(response.getId()).isEqualTo(bookingId);\\n    assertThat(response.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\\n    assertThat(response.getStatus()).isEqualTo(\\\"CONFIRMED\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Happy Path — Request DTO to Domain\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_create_request_to_booking_domain_with_correct_fields() {\\n    UUID userId = UUID.randomUUID();\\n    List<UUID> seatIds = List.of(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID());\\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\\n\\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\\n\\n    assertThat(booking.getUserId()).isEqualTo(userId);\\n    assertThat(booking.getSeatIds()).containsExactlyElementsOf(seatIds);\\n    assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.PENDING);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Edge Case — Null / Optional Fields\\n\\n```java\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_null_cancellation_reason_to_null_in_response() {\\n    Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\\n    booking.setCancellationReason(null);\\n\\n    BookingResponse response = mapper.toResponse(booking);\\n\\n    assertThat(response.getCancellationReason()).isNull();\\n}\\n\\n@Test\\nvoid should_map_optional_notes_field_when_present() {\\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds);\\n    request.setNotes(\\\"Wheelchair access required\\\");\\n\\n    Booking booking = mapper.toDomain(request);\\n\\n    assertThat(booking.getNotes()).isEqualTo(\\\"Wheelchair access required\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference — Which Test Goes Where?\\n\\nUse this table to decide the correct layer for any test scenario:\\n\\n| Scenario | Correct Layer | Reason |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `booking.confirm()` succeeds when status is PENDING | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\\n| `booking.confirm()` throws when status is CANCELLED | `domain/` | Pure domain rule |\\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` saves and publishes event | `service/` | Orchestration |\\n| `BookingService.confirmBooking()` throws when booking not found | `service/` | Orchestration — entity lookup |\\n| `BookingService.initiateBooking()` calls PaymentAclService | `service/` | Orchestration — ACL call |\\n| `BookingService` refunds when DB write fails after payment | `service/` | Compensation logic |\\n| `GET /api/bookings/{id}` returns 200 with correct JSON | `api/` | HTTP contract |\\n| `POST /api/bookings` returns 400 for missing seatIds | `api/` | Request validation |\\n| `DELETE /api/bookings/{id}` returns 403 without admin role | `api/` | Authorization |\\n| No class in `booking.domain` imports a `@Repository` | `arch/` | Boundary rule |\\n| No class in `booking` imports `payments.repository` | `arch/` | Cross-module boundary |\\n| Events in `booking.event.published` carry no `@Entity` | `arch/` | Event payload rule |\\n| `BookingMapper.toResponse()` maps all fields correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\\n| `BookingMapper.toDomain()` maps request to domain correctly | `mapper/` | Translation contract |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\\n\\n| Mistake | Why It's Wrong | Fix |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Testing `booking.confirm()` logic in `service/` tests | Re-tests domain rules — adds no value | Move to `domain/` |\\n| Using `@SpringBootTest` in `service/` or `domain/` tests | Loads entire context — massively slow | Use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` or no annotation |\\n| Putting `@MockBean` in `domain/` tests | If you need a mock, domain has an infra dependency | Remove the dependency from the domain class |\\n| Testing business logic in `api/` tests | Controller tests should only care about HTTP contract | Mock the service; move logic test to `service/` or `domain/` |\\n| Deleting or weakening an `arch/` rule to make a test pass | Hides a real architectural violation | Fix the production code, not the test |\\n| Using `assertThat(response).isNotNull()` as the only assertion | Proves nothing — a null check is not a contract | Assert every field that matters to the caller |\\n| Missing event payload assertions in `service/` tests | A wrong payload breaks the consuming module silently | Use `ArgumentCaptor` to assert every event field |\\n| Mapping fields inside `service/` or `controller/` tests | Indicates production code is mapping in the wrong place | All mapping belongs in `mapper/` — fix the source |\\n\"\n"
  }
}
```

false)\n    private Long cityId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"title\", nullable = false)\n    private String title;\n\n    @Column(name = \"description\")\n    private String description;\n\n    @Column(name = \"start_time\", nullable = false)\n    private ZonedDateTime startTime;\n\n    @Column(name = \"end_time\", nullable = false)\n    private ZonedDateTime endTime;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"seating_mode\", nullable = false)\n    private SeatingMode seatingMode;\n\n    @Column(name = \"capacity\", nullable = false)\n    private Integer capacity;\n\n    @Column(name = \"source_seatmap_id\")\n    private String sourceSeatMapId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"is_recurring\", nullable = false)\n    private boolean recurring;\n\n    @Column(name = \"recurrence_rule\")\n    private String recurrenceRule;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"status\", nullable = false)\n    private ShowSlotStatus status = ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT;\n\n    @Column(name = \"eb_event_id\")\n    private String ebEventId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"eb_series_id\")\n    private String ebSeriesId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"sync_attempt_count\", nullable = false)\n    private int syncAttemptCount;\n\n    @Column(name = \"last_sync_error\")\n    private String lastSyncError;\n\n    @Column(name = \"last_attempted_at\")\n    private Instant lastAttemptedAt;\n\n    @OneToMany(mappedBy = \"slot\", cascade = CascadeType.ALL, orphanRemoval = true)\n    private List<ShowSlotPricingTier> pricingTiers = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    @OneToMany(mappedBy = \"parentSlot\", cascade = CascadeType.ALL, orphanRemoval = true)\n    private List<ShowSlotOccurrence> occurrences = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    protected ShowSlot() {\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlot(\n        Long organizationId,\n        Long venueId,\n        Long cityId,\n        String title,\n        String description,\n        ZonedDateTime startTime,\n        ZonedDateTime endTime,\n        SeatingMode seatingMode,\n        Integer capacity,\n        boolean recurring,\n        String recurrenceRule,\n        String sourceSeatMapId\n    ) {\n        this.organizationId = organizationId;\n        this.venueId = venueId;\n        this.cityId = cityId;\n        this.title = title;\n        this.description = description;\n        this.startTime = startTime;\n        this.endTime = endTime;\n        this.seatingMode = seatingMode;\n        this.capacity = capacity;\n        this.recurring = recurring;\n        this.recurrenceRule = recurrenceRule;\n        this.sourceSeatMapId = sourceSeatMapId;\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT;\n        this.syncAttemptCount = 0;\n    }\n\n    public Long getOrganizationId() {\n        return organizationId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getVenueId() {\n        return venueId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getCityId() {\n        return cityId;\n    }\n\n    public String getTitle() {\n        return title;\n    }\n\n    public String getDescription() {\n        return description;\n    }\n\n    public ZonedDateTime getStartTime() {\n        return startTime;\n    }\n\n    public ZonedDateTime getEndTime() {\n        return endTime;\n    }\n\n    public SeatingMode getSeatingMode() {\n        return seatingMode;\n    }\n\n    public Integer getCapacity() {\n        return capacity;\n    }\n\n    public String getSourceSeatMapId() {\n        return sourceSeatMapId;\n    }\n\n    public boolean isRecurring() {\n        return recurring;\n    }\n\n    public String getRecurrenceRule() {\n        return recurrenceRule;\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlotStatus getStatus() {\n        return status;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbEventId() {\n        return ebEventId;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbSeriesId() {\n        return ebSeriesId;\n    }\n\n    public int getSyncAttemptCount() {\n        return syncAttemptCount;\n    }\n\n    public String getLastSyncError() {\n        return lastSyncError;\n    }\n\n    public Instant getLastAttemptedAt() {\n        return lastAttemptedAt;\n    }\n\n    public List<ShowSlotPricingTier> getPricingTiers() {\n        return pricingTiers;\n    }\n\n    public List<ShowSlotOccurrence> getOccurrences() {\n        return occurrences;\n    }\n\n    public void addPricingTier(ShowSlotPricingTier tier) {\n        pricingTiers.add(tier);\n        tier.attachTo(this);\n    }\n\n    public void clearPricingTiers() {\n        pricingTiers.forEach(tier -> tier.attachTo(null));\n        pricingTiers.clear();\n    }\n\n    public void addOccurrence(ShowSlotOccurrence occurrence) {\n        occurrences.add(occurrence);\n        occurrence.attachTo(this);\n    }\n\n    public void submit() {\n        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> PENDING_SYNC\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n        }\n        if (pricingTiers == null || pricingTiers.isEmpty()) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot must have at least one pricing tier before submission\", \"MISSING_PRICING_TIERS\");\n        }\n        markPendingSync();\n    }\n\n    public void activate() {\n        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> ACTIVE\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n        }\n        markActive();\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot already cancelled\", \"SLOT_ALREADY_CANCELLED\");\n        }\n        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"DRAFT slots must be deleted, not cancelled\", \"SLOT_DRAFT_CANCEL\");\n        }\n        markCancelled();\n    }\n\n    public void markPendingSync() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC;\n    }\n\n    public void markActive() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE;\n        this.syncAttemptCount = 0;\n        this.lastSyncError = null;\n        this.lastAttemptedAt = null;\n    }\n\n    public void markCancelled() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void recordSyncFailure(String error) {\n        this.syncAttemptCount++;\n        this.lastSyncError = error;\n        this.lastAttemptedAt = Instant.now();\n    }\n\n"
  }
}

getCapacity() {\n        return capacity;\n    }\n\n    public String getSourceSeatMapId() {\n        return sourceSeatMapId;\n    }\n\n    public boolean isRecurring() {\n        return recurring;\n    }\n\n    public String getRecurrenceRule() {\n        return recurrenceRule;\n    }\n\n    public ShowSlotStatus getStatus() {\n        return status;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbEventId() {\n        return ebEventId;\n    }\n\n    public String getEbSeriesId() {\n        return ebSeriesId;\n    }\n\n    public int getSyncAttemptCount() {\n        return syncAttemptCount;\n    }\n\n    public String getLastSyncError() {\n        return lastSyncError;\n    }\n\n    public Instant getLastAttemptedAt() {\n        return lastAttemptedAt;\n    }\n\n    public List<ShowSlotPricingTier> getPricingTiers() {\n        return pricingTiers;\n    }\n\n    public List<ShowSlotOccurrence> getOccurrences() {\n        return occurrences;\n    }\n\n    public void addPricingTier(ShowSlotPricingTier tier) {\n        pricingTiers.add(tier);\n        tier.attachTo(this);\n    }\n\n    public void clearPricingTiers() {\n        pricingTiers.forEach(tier -> tier.attachTo(null));\n        pricingTiers.clear();\n    }\n\n    public void addOccurrence(ShowSlotOccurrence occurrence) {\n        occurrences.add(occurrence);\n        occurrence.attachTo(this);\n    }\n\n    public void submit() {\n        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> PENDING_SYNC\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n        }\n        if (pricingTiers == null || pricingTiers.isEmpty()) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot must have at least one pricing tier before submission\", \"MISSING_PRICING_TIERS\");\n        }\n        markPendingSync();\n    }\n\n    public void activate() {\n        if (status != ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid slot transition: \" + status + \" -> ACTIVE\", \"INVALID_SLOT_TRANSITION\");\n        }\n        markActive();\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Slot already cancelled\", \"SLOT_ALREADY_CANCELLED\");\n        }\n        if (status == ShowSlotStatus.DRAFT) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"DRAFT slots must be deleted, not cancelled\", \"SLOT_DRAFT_CANCEL\");\n        }\n        markCancelled();\n    }\n\n    public void markPendingSync() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC;\n    }\n\n    public void markActive() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.ACTIVE;\n        this.syncAttemptCount = 0;\n        this.lastSyncError = null;\n        this.lastAttemptedAt = null;\n    }\n\n    public void markCancelled() {\n        this.status = ShowSlotStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void recordSyncFailure(String error) {\n        this.syncAttemptCount++;\n        this.lastSyncError = error;\n        this.lastAttemptedAt = Instant.now();\n    }\n\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 136
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:14:04.903Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:14:04.903Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_p7oiIy7TDBk1a7nfp4BWZnsO",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 68b129\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2963\nOutput:\n# SPEC_AUTH.MD — JWT Authentication Infrastructure (shared/security/)\n\n## 1. Overview\n\nThis spec covers the **shared JWT security infrastructure** that all modules depend on for authentication. It defines the three components that live in `shared/security/`: `JwtTokenProvider`, `JwtAuthenticationFilter`, and the updated `SecurityConfig`. It does **not** cover identity module endpoints — those are in `SPEC_3.MD`.\n\n**Consumers:** Every module that has JWT-protected endpoints (identity, booking-inventory, payments-ticketing, etc.)  \n**Tech stack:** Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21, Spring Security 6, JJWT (`io.jsonwebtoken:jjwt-api`), RS256 (RSA-2048)  \n**Scope:** v1.0 — Shared JWT infrastructure layer\n\n---\n\n## 2. Goals & Non-Goals\n\n### Goals\n- Replace the existing `httpBasic()` in `SecurityConfig` with a stateless JWT filter chain.\n- Provide `JwtTokenProvider` for signing and validating access JWTs using RS256.\n- Provide `JwtAuthenticationFilter` (`OncePerRequestFilter`) that validates incoming JWTs and populates `SecurityContextHolder`.\n- Provide `BCryptPasswordEncoder` and `AuthenticationManager` beans in `SecurityConfig`.\n- Define the RSA key pair loading strategy (PEM files on classpath).\n- Document the DI wiring for `UserDetailsServiceImpl` (lives in identity, wired at runtime).\n\n### Non-Goals\n- Token issuance logic (login endpoint) — lives in `identity/service/AuthService.java`.\n- Refresh token handling — lives in identity module.\n- Role-based access control rules beyond `authenticated()` — out of scope for v1.0.\n- OAuth2 / OIDC resource server setup.\n- Key rotation or key management service integration.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Architectural Position\n\n```\napp/ (entry point)\n  └── shared/security/         ← THIS SPEC — JWT infra only\n        ├── JwtTokenProvider   ← sign + validate JWTs using RSA private/public key\n        ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter ← reads Authorization header, validates token, sets context\n        └── SecurityConfig     ← filter chain, public endpoints, PasswordEncoder, AuthManager\n              ↑\n              DI wires UserDetailsServiceImpl at runtime (bean from identity module)\n              ↑\n        identity/service/UserDetailsServiceImpl  ← implements UserDetailsService, loads by email\n              ↑\n        identity/repository/UserRepository       ← DB lookup\n```\n\n**Key rule:** `shared/` has no dependency on any module. `UserDetailsServiceImpl` is a bean provided by the `identity` module and injected into Spring Security's `AuthenticationProvider` through standard Spring DI. `shared/security/` never imports any identity class directly.\n\n---\n\n## 4. File Structure\n\n### New / Modified Files\n\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/\n├── JwtTokenProvider.java          ← NEW — RS256 sign, validate, extract claims\n├── JwtAuthenticationFilter.java   ← NEW — OncePerRequestFilter, JWT → SecurityContext\n├── SecurityConfig.java            ← MODIFY — replace httpBasic(); add JWT filter + beans\n└── Roles.java                     ← EXISTING — no changes needed\n\napp/src/main/resources/\n└── certs/\n    ├── private.pem                ← NEW — RSA-2048 private key (PKCS#8, PEM, NOT committed to git)\n    └── public.pem                 ← NEW — RSA-2048 public key (PEM)\n```\n\n### Identity File (out of scope for this spec, listed for wiring reference)\n```\nidentity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/service/\n└── UserDetailsServiceImpl.java    ← implements UserDetailsService; loads User by email from UserRepository\n```\n\n### Maven Dependency Changes\nAdd to `shared/pom.xml`:\n```xml\n<dependency>\n    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>\n    <artifactId>jjwt-api</artifactId>\n    <version>0.12.6</version>\n</dependency>\n<dependency>\n    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>\n    <artifactId>jjwt-impl</artifactId>\n    <version>0.12.6</version>\n    <scope>runtime</scope>\n</dependency>\n<dependency>\n    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>\n    <artifactId>jjwt-jackson</artifactId>\n    <version>0.12.6</version>\n    <scope>runtime</scope>\n</dependency>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. RSA Key Pair\n\n### Algorithm\n- **RS256** (RSA + SHA-256): private key signs, public key verifies\n- Key size: **RSA-2048** minimum\n- Format: PEM (PKCS#8 for private, X.509/SPKI for public)\n\n### Key Generation (one-time, run locally)\n```bash\n# Generate RSA-2048 private key (PKCS#8)\nopenssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:2048 -out private.pem\n\n# Extract public key\nopenssl rsa -pubout -in private.pem -out public.pem\n```\n\nPlace both files under `app/src/main/resources/certs/`. Add `certs/private.pem` to `.gitignore` — never commit the private key.\n\n### Configuration Properties\nIn `app/src/main/resources/application.properties`:\n```properties\njwt.private-key-path=classpath:certs/private.pem\njwt.public-key-path=classpath:certs/public.pem\n```\n\nIn test environments (`application-test.properties`), generate a fresh key pair per test run or use a fixed test key pair committed under `src/test/resources/certs/`.\n\n---\n\n## 6. JwtTokenProvider\n\n**Location:** `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/JwtTokenProvider.java`  \n**Type:** `@Component`\n\n### Responsibilities\n1. Load RSA private key and public key from configured PEM paths on startup (`@PostConstruct`).\n2. `generateToken(Long userId, String role)` → signed JWT string (RS256).\n3. `validateToken(String token)` → `boolean` — returns `false` (never throws) on any failure.\n4. `extractUserId(String token)` → `Long` — extracts `sub` claim.\n5. `extractRole(String token)` → `String` — extracts `role` claim.\n\n### JWT Claim Contract (from SPEC_3.MD Section 8)\n\n| Claim | Value | Notes |\n|-------|-------|-------|\n| `sub` | `userId` as String | BIGSERIAL Long, consistent with all schema PKs |\n| `role` | `\"USER\"` | String, not `ROLE_USER` — no Spring prefix |\n| `iat` | issued-at epoch seconds | set by JJWT automatically |\n| `exp` | iat + 3600 (1 hour) | access token TTL |\n| `jti` | `UUID.randomUUID().toString()` | unique per token for future revocation |\n| `typ` | `\"access\"` | differentiates from future token types |\n\n### Design Notes\n- `@Value(\"${jwt.private-key-path}\")` and `@Value(\"${jwt.public-key-path}\")` inject paths.\n- `@PostConstruct init()` loads PEM bytes from `ResourceLoader`, parses to `PrivateKey` and `PublicKey` using `java.security.KeyFactory`.\n- Use `Jwts.builder()` with `.signWith(privateKey, Jwts.SIG.RS256)` (JJWT 0.12.x API).\n- `validateToken` wraps `Jwts.parser().verifyWith(publicKey).build().parseSignedClaims(token)` in a try/catch; returns `false` on `JwtException` or `IllegalArgumentException`.\n- No static state — all fields are instance fields injected at startup.\n\n### PEM Parsing Utility (inside JwtTokenProvider)\n```\nPrivate key: strip \"-----BEGIN/END PRIVATE KEY-----\", Base64 decode, KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePrivate(new PKCS8EncodedKeySpec(bytes))\nPublic key:  strip \"-----BEGIN/END PUBLIC KEY-----\", Base64 decode, KeyFactory.getInstance(\"RSA\").generatePublic(new X509EncodedKeySpec(bytes))\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. JwtAuthenticationFilter\n\n**Location:** `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java`  \n**Type:** `@Component`, extends `OncePerRequestFilter`\n\n### Responsibilities\n1. Extract JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.\n2. If token absent or malformed header format → call `filterChain.doFilter()` and return (no exception, Spring Security's entry point will return 401 for protected routes).\n3. Call `JwtTokenProvider.validateToken(token)`.\n4. If valid → extract `userId` and `role`, build `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` with `role` authority, set into `SecurityContextHolder`.\n5. If invalid → clear `SecurityContextHolder` and continue filter chain (Spring Security handles the 401).\n\n### Filter Logic (pseudocode)\n```\ndoFilterInternal(request, response, chain):\n  header = request.getHeader(\"Authorization\")\n  if header == null or !header.startsWith(\"Bearer \"):\n    chain.doFilter(request, response)\n    return\n\n  token = header.substring(7)\n  if jwtTokenProvider.validateToken(token):\n    userId = jwtTokenProvider.extractUserId(token)\n    role   = jwtTokenProvider.extractRole(token)\n    auth   = new UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken(\n               userId,                          // principal = Long userId\n               null,                            // credentials = null (already verified)\n               List.of(new SimpleGrantedAuthority(\"ROLE_\" + role))\n             )\n    auth.setDetails(new WebAuthenticationDetailsSource().buildDetails(request))\n    SecurityContextHolder.getContext().setAuthentication(auth)\n  else:\n    SecurityContextHolder.clearContext()\n\n  chain.doFilter(request, response)\n```\n\n### Authority Naming\nJJWT stores `role` as `\"USER\"` in the claim. Spring Security expects authorities prefixed with `ROLE_`. The filter applies the prefix: `\"ROLE_\" + role`. This means `@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")` in controllers will work without any prefix on the claim value.\n\n---\n\n## 8. SecurityConfig (Updated)\n\n**Location:** `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/security/SecurityConfig.java`  \n**Current state:** Uses `.httpBasic()` — must be replaced entirely.\n\n### Required Beans\n| Bean | Class | Why |\n|------|-------|-----|\n| `PasswordEncoder` | `BCryptPasswordEncoder` | Used by `AuthService` for hashing and matching |\n| `AuthenticationManager` | via `AuthenticationConfiguration` | Used by `AuthService` for credential validation during login |\n| `SecurityFilterChain` | — | Full JWT chain replacing httpBasic |\n\n### Public Endpoints (no JWT required)\nAll existing public endpoints from FR1/FR2 **plus** the FR3 auth endpoints:\n\n```\n/api/v1/catalog/**                            ← existing (FR1)\n/api/v1/admin/orgs/*/eventbrite/callback      ← existing (FR1)\n/admin/v1/webhooks/eventbrite                 ← existing (FR1)\n/api/v1/auth/register                         ← FR3\n/api/v1/auth/login                            ← FR3\n/api/v1/auth/token/refresh                    ← FR3\n/api/v1/auth/password/reset                   ← FR3\n/api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm           ← FR3\n```\n\nAll other requests require a valid JWT in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.\n\n### Filter Chain Configuration\n```\nhttp\n  .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable())\n  .sessionManagement(session -> session\n      .sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))\n  .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth\n      .requestMatchers(<public endpoints above>).permitAll()\n      .anyRequest().authenticated()\n  )\n  .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthenticationFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class)\n```\n\n**No `.httpBasic()`. No `.formLogin()`. No `.sessionManagement()` with HTTP sessions.**\n\n### DI Wiring Note — UserDetailsService\n`UserDetailsServiceImpl` is a `@Service` bean defined in the `identity` module. It implements Spring Security's `UserDetailsService` interface. Spring Boot's auto-configuration for `DaoAuthenticationProvider` picks up any `UserDetailsService` bean on the classpath automatically — no explicit wiring needed in `SecurityConfig`. Since this is a modular monolith with a single `app/` entry point, all beans from all modules are loaded into the same `ApplicationContext`. The `PasswordEncoder` bean in `SecurityConfig` is also auto-detected by the same `DaoAuthenticationProvider`.\n\n**`SecurityConfig` never imports any class from `identity/`.** The wiring is purely through interface contracts (`UserDetailsService`, `PasswordEncoder`).\n\n---\n\n## 9. Error Handling\n\nThe filter never throws exceptions or writes the response directly. Spring Security's default `AuthenticationEntryPoint` handles the 401 response when an unauthenticated request hits a protected endpoint.\n"
  }
}
```

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:14:11.563Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_C2KiPNs8exaGjw5bnAHTPjCl",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 46aeca\nWall time: 0.0512 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 751\nOutput:\n-- owner: scheduling module\nCREATE TABLE show_slot (\n    id                    BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\n    organization_id       BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    venue_id              BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    city_id               BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n    title                 VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,\n    description           TEXT,\n    start_time            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL,\n    end_time              TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL,\n    seating_mode          VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL\n                           CONSTRAINT chk_slot_seating_mode CHECK (seating_mode IN ('GA', 'RESERVED')),\n    capacity              INTEGER      NOT NULL,\n    source_seatmap_id     VARCHAR(255),\n    is_recurring          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,\n    recurrence_rule       TEXT,\n    status                VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT'\n                           CONSTRAINT chk_slot_status CHECK (status IN ('DRAFT', 'PENDING_SYNC', 'ACTIVE', 'CANCELLED')),\n    eb_event_id           VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,\n    eb_series_id          VARCHAR(255),\n    sync_attempt_count    INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    last_sync_error       TEXT,\n    last_attempted_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,\n    created_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    updated_at            TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_org   FOREIGN KEY (organization_id) REFERENCES organization(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_venue FOREIGN KEY (venue_id)        REFERENCES venue(id)        ON DELETE RESTRICT,\n    CONSTRAINT fk_show_slot_city  FOREIGN KEY (city_id)         REFERENCES city(id)         ON DELETE RESTRICT,\n    CONSTRAINT chk_slot_times     CHECK (end_time > start_time),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_slot_capacity  CHECK (capacity > 0)\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_org_id     ON show_slot(organization_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_venue_id   ON show_slot(venue_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_city_id    ON show_slot(city_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_status     ON show_slot(status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_eb_event   ON show_slot(eb_event_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_venue_time ON show_slot(venue_id, start_time, end_time);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_active_eb  ON show_slot(status, eb_event_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_show_slot_org_city   ON show_slot(organization_id, city_id);\n---\n-- owner: discovery-catalog module\nCREATE TABLE city (\n    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n    organization_id BIGINT NOT NULL,\n    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    description VARCHAR(500),\n    state VARCHAR(10),\n    country_code VARCHAR(2),\n    latitude VARCHAR(50),\n    longitude VARCHAR(50),\n    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n    CONSTRAINT fk_city_org FOREIGN KEY (organization_id) REFERENCES organization(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_city_org_id ON city(organization_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_city_name ON city(name);\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uk_org_city_name ON city(organization_id, name);\n"
  }
}

{

    "output": "Chunk ID: 6db972\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 722\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.scheduling.repository;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlot;\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.enums.ShowSlotStatus;\nimport java.time.ZonedDateTime;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.Page;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.Pageable;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaSpecificationExecutor;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.Query;\nimport org.springframework.data.repository.query.Param;\n\npublic interface ShowSlotRepository extends JpaRepository<ShowSlot, Long>, JpaSpecificationExecutor<ShowSlot> {\n\n    @Query(\"select s from ShowSlot s where s.venueId = :venueId and s.status <> 'CANCELLED' and s.startTime < :endTime and s.endTime > :startTime\")\n    List<ShowSlot> findConflicts(\n        @Param(\"venueId\") Long venueId,\n        @Param(\"startTime\") ZonedDateTime startTime,\n        @Param(\"endTime\") ZonedDateTime endTime\n    );\n\n    @Query(\"select s from ShowSlot s where s.venueId = :venueId and s.status <> 'CANCELLED' and s.startTime < :endTime and s.endTime > :startTime and (:excludeId is null or s.id <> :excludeId)\")\n    List<ShowSlot> findConflictsExcludingId(\n        @Param(\"venueId\") Long venueId,\n        @Param(\"startTime\") ZonedDateTime startTime,\n        @Param(\"endTime\") ZonedDateTime endTime,\n        @Param(\"excludeId\") Long excludeId\n    );\n\n    @Query(\"select s from ShowSlot s where s.venueId = :venueId and s.status <> 'CANCELLED' and s.endTime <= :startTime and (:excludeId is null or s.id <> :excludeId) order by s.endTime desc\")\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findPrevSlotForGap(\n        @Param(\"venueId\") Long venueId,\n        @Param(\"startTime\") ZonedDateTime startTime,\n        @Param(\"excludeId\") Long excludeId\n    );\n\n    @Query(\"select s from ShowSlot s where s.venueId = :venueId and s.status <> 'CANCELLED' and s.startTime >= :endTime and (:excludeId is null or s.id <> :excludeId) order by s.startTime asc\")\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findNextSlotForGap(\n        @Param(\"venueId\") Long venueId,\n        @Param(\"endTime\") ZonedDateTime endTime,\n        @Param(\"excludeId\") Long excludeId\n    );\n\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findFirstByVenueIdAndEndTimeLessThanEqualOrderByEndTimeDesc(Long venueId, ZonedDateTime startTime);\n\n    Optional<ShowSlot> findFirstByVenueIdAndStartTimeGreaterThanEqualOrderByStartTimeAsc(Long venueId, ZonedDateTime endTime);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatus(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Pageable pageable);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndSyncAttemptCountGreaterThan(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, int attempts, Pageable pageable);\n\n    Page<ShowSlot> findByOrganizationIdAndStatusAndLastSyncErrorIsNotNull(Long organizationId, ShowSlotStatus status, Pageable pageable);\n}\n"
  }
}

ShowSlotController {\n\n    private final ShowSlotService showSlotService;\n    private final ShowSlotMapper showSlotMapper;\n\n    public ShowSlotController(ShowSlotService showSlotService,
