 @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotResponse createSlot(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @Valid @RequestBody CreateShowSlotRequest request\n    ) {\n        ShowSlot slot = showSlotService.createSlot(organizationId, request);\n        return showSlotMapper.toResponse(slot);\n    }\n\n    @PostMapping(\"/{id}/submit\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotSubmitResponse submitSlot(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId\n    ) {\n        ShowSlot slot = showSlotService.submitSlot(organizationId, slotId);\n        return new ShowSlotSubmitResponse(slot.getId(), slot.getStatus(), slot.getEbEventId(), slot.getSyncAttemptCount(), \"EB sync initiated\");\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/{id}\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotResponse getSlot(@PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId) {\n        return showSlotMapper.toResponse(showSlotService.getSlot(slotId));\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public PageResponse<ShowSlotResponse> listSlots(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"status\", required = false) ShowSlotStatus status,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"venueId\", required = false) Long venueId,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"cityId\", required = false) Long cityId,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"startAfter\", required = false) @DateTimeFormat(iso = DateTimeFormat.ISO.DATE_TIME) ZonedDateTime startAfter,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"page\", defaultValue = \"0\") int page,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"size\", defaultValue = \"20\") int size\n    ) {\n        Page<ShowSlot> slots = showSlotService.listSlots(organizationId, status, venueId, cityId, startAfter, page, size);\n        List<ShowSlotResponse> responses = slots.map(showSlotMapper::toResponse).getContent();\n        return new PageResponse<>(responses, slots.getTotalElements(), page, size);\n    }\n\n    @PutMapping(\"/{id}\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ResponseEntity<ShowSlotResponse> updateSlot(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId,\n        @Valid @RequestBody UpdateShowSlotRequest request\n    ) {\n        ShowSlotUpdateResult result = showSlotService.updateSlot(organizationId, slotId, request);\n        ShowSlotResponse response = showSlotMapper.toResponse(result.slot());\n        if (result.ebSyncFailed()) {\n            return ResponseEntity.status(207).body(response);\n        }\n        return ResponseEntity.ok(response);\n    }\n\n    @PostMapping(\"/{id}/cancel\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotCancelResponse cancelSlot(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId\n    ) {\n        boolean ebCancelled = showSlotService.cancelSlot(organizationId, slotId);\n        ShowSlot slot = showSlotService.getSlot(slotId);\n        return new ShowSlotCancelResponse(slot.getId(), slot.getStatus(), ebCancelled, \"Slot cancelled\");\n    }\n\n    @PostMapping(\"/{id}/retry-sync\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotRetryResponse retrySync(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId\n    ) {\n        showSlotService.retrySync(organizationId, slotId);\n        return new ShowSlotRetryResponse(ShowSlotStatus.PENDING_SYNC, \"Retry initiated\");\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/{id}/occurrences\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public ShowSlotOccurrenceListResponse listOccurrences(@PathVariable(\"id\") Long slotId) {\n        List<ShowSlotOccurrence> occurrences = showSlotService.listOccurrences(slotId);\n        List<ShowSlotOccurrenceResponse> responseList = occurrences.stream()\n            .map(showSlotMapper::toOccurrenceResponse)\n            .toList();\n        return new ShowSlotOccurrenceListResponse(slotId, responseList);\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/mismatches\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n    public PageResponse<ShowSlotResponse> listMismatches(\n        @RequestParam(\"orgId\") Long organizationId,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"page\", defaultValue = \"0\") int page,\n        @RequestParam(name = \"size\", defaultValue = \"20\") int size\n    ) {\n        Page<ShowSlot> slots = showSlotService.listMismatches(organizationId, page, size);\n        List<ShowSlotResponse> responses = slots.map(showSlotMapper::toResponse).getContent();\n        return new PageResponse<>(responses, slots.getTotalElements(), page, size);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

\"java(mapPricingTiers(slot.getPricingTiers()))\")\n    ShowSlotResponse toResponse(ShowSlot slot);\n\n    ShowSlotOccurrenceResponse toOccurrenceResponse(ShowSlotOccurrence occurrence);\n\n    @Mapping(target = \"priceAmount\", source = \"price.amount\")\n    @Mapping(target = \"currency\", source = \"price.currency\")\n    ShowSlotPricingTierResponse toPricingTierResponse(ShowSlotPricingTier tier);\n\n    default List<ShowSlotPricingTierResponse> mapPricingTiers(List<ShowSlotPricingTier> tiers) {\n        return tiers == null ? List.of() : tiers.stream().map(this::toPricingTierResponse).toList();\n    }\n\n    default ShowSlot toEntity(Long organizationId, Long venueId, Long cityId, CreateShowSlotRequest request) {\n        return new ShowSlot(\n            organizationId,\n            venueId,\n            cityId,\n            request.title(),\n            request.description(),\n            request.startTime(),\n            request.endTime(),\n            request.seatingMode(),\n            request.capacity(),\n            Boolean.TRUE.equals(request.isRecurring()),\n            request.recurrenceRule(),\n            request.sourceSeatMapId()\n        );\n    }\n\n    default List<ShowSlotPricingTier> toPricingTiers(List<PricingTierRequest> tiers) {\n        if (tiers == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return tiers.stream().map(this::toPricingTier).toList();\n    }\n\n    default ShowSlotPricingTier toPricingTier(PricingTierRequest request) {\n        Money price = new Money(request.priceAmount(), request.currency());\n        TierType type = request.tierType();\n        return new ShowSlotPricingTier(request.name(), price, request.quota(), type);\n    }\n\n    default ConflictAlternativeDto toConflictDto(ConflictAlternativeResponse response) {\n        return new ConflictAlternativeDto(\n            mapTimeWindows(response.sameVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapVenues(response.nearbyVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapTimeWindows(response.adjustedTimeOptions())\n        );\n    }\n\n    default List<TimeWindowOptionDto> mapTimeWindows(List<TimeWindowOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toTimeWindowDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default TimeWindowOptionDto toTimeWindowDto(TimeWindowOption option) {\n        return new TimeWindowOptionDto(option.proposedStart(), option.proposedEnd());\n    }\n\n    default List<VenueOptionDto> mapVenues(List<VenueOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toVenueDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default VenueOptionDto toVenueDto(VenueOption option) {\n        return new VenueOptionDto(option.venueId(), option.venueName(), option.city(), option.capacity());\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

default ConflictAlternativeDto toConflictDto(ConflictAlternativeResponse response) {\n        return new ConflictAlternativeDto(\n            mapTimeWindows(response.sameVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapVenues(response.nearbyVenueAlternatives()),\n            mapTimeWindows(response.adjustedTimeOptions())\n        );\n    }\n\n    default List<TimeWindowOptionDto> mapTimeWindows(List<TimeWindowOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toTimeWindowDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default TimeWindowOptionDto toTimeWindowDto(TimeWindowOption option) {\n        return new TimeWindowOptionDto(option.proposedStart(), option.proposedEnd());\n    }\n\n    default List<VenueOptionDto> mapVenues(List<VenueOption> options) {\n        if (options == null) {\n            return List.of();\n        }\n        return options.stream().map(this::toVenueDto).toList();\n    }\n\n    default VenueOptionDto toVenueDto(VenueOption option) {\n        return new VenueOptionDto(option.venueId(), option.venueName(), option.city(), option.capacity());\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

{

    "call_id": "call_W9c7kdQVuXAkHkVK8OIod0qz",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 90fdf9\nWall time: 0.0516 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 829\nOutput:\n32:MapStruct:     1.5.x\n352:- Never map fields manually in a service or controller — always delegate to a `@Mapper` class\n435:- **Always use `Page<T>` and `Pageable` for any list endpoint** exposed via the API — never return unbounded `List<T>` from a controller.\n436:- Accept `Pageable` as a controller parameter via `@PageableDefault` — Spring auto-binds `?page=0&size=20&sort=createdAt,desc`.\n438:- Return `Page<ResponseDTO>` from the controller, wrapped in `ApiResponse`.\n441:// ✅ Correct paginated controller\n443:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n466:MapStruct interfaces. Pure translation — no business logic, no service calls, no conditions\n507:### Controller Layer (`api/controller/`)\n513:- Use `@PreAuthorize` for role-based access — never check roles in service or domain\n514:- Validate request body with `@Valid` — never manually validate in controller code\n516:- Never catch exceptions in the controller — let `GlobalExceptionHandler` handle them\n518:- Never define `@ExceptionHandler` in a controller — use `GlobalExceptionHandler` only\n521:// ✅ Correct controller\n532:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n541:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n551:    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n717:- `StateMachineService` is the only class that calls the state machine — never call it from a controller\n768:- Tool definitions are registered in the service layer, not in the controller\n843:| `JwtAuthenticationFilter` | `shared/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter` | Validates JWT, populates `SecurityContext` |\n845:| `CurrentUserArgumentResolver` | `shared/security/CurrentUserArgumentResolver` | Resolves `@CurrentUser` annotation in controllers |\n848:### Accessing the Authenticated User in Controllers\n850:Never call `SecurityContextHolder.getContext()` directly in controllers or services. Use the `@CurrentUser` annotation resolved by `CurrentUserArgumentResolver`.\n853:// ✅ Correct — @CurrentUser injected by resolver\n855:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n857:        @CurrentUser UserPrincipal principal,\n867:        .getAuthentication().getPrincipal(); // never do this in a service\n872:### Authorization Rules\n875:- Method-level security uses `@PreAuthorize` on controller methods — never on service methods\n877:- Never hardcode role strings outside of `@PreAuthorize` annotations — define constants in `shared/security/Roles`\n881:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.ADMIN + \"')\")\n884:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('ADMIN')\")\n898:Never add module-specific claims to the JWT. If a service needs additional user context, fetch it via the `identity` module REST API.\n933:| Never on controllers | Controllers never have `@Transactional` |\n943:// ✅ Correct Springdoc annotations on controller\n953:@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\n1012:- [ ] No field mapping in services or controllers — all in `mapper/`\n1013:- [ ] **Mapper ownership: services use mapper for request DTO → domain; controllers use mapper for domain → response DTO. Neither maps manually.**\n1029:- [ ] **All list-returning API endpoints use `Page<T>` and accept `Pageable` — no unbounded `List<T>` from controllers**\n1030:- [ ] **`@CurrentUser UserPrincipal` used in controllers — never `SecurityContextHolder` in services**\n1031:- [ ] **`@PreAuthorize` on controller methods only — never on service methods**\n"
  }
}
```

    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "update_plan",
    "arguments": "{\"explanation\":\"Implementing SPEC_3 requires schema, domain, service, API, and wiring across the empty identity module. I’ll build it incrementally and validate with a compile/test run.\",\"plan\":[{\"step\":\"Inspect current project conventions and identify required identity/security/migration touchpoints for SPEC_3\", \"status\":\"completed\"},{\"step\":\"Implement identity module core domain, repositories, DTOs, mappers, services, controllers, and module-specific exceptions per SPEC_3\", \"status\":\"in_progress\"},{\"step\":\"Add Flyway migrations V10–V16 and align module build dependencies/config for JPA/security/MapStruct\", \"status\":\"pending\"},{\"step\":\"Build/verify with Maven and fix compile issues\", \"status\":\"pending\"}]}",
    "call_id": "call_pqBFHXVy6f5BIEQmIkC770wL"
  }
}
```

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/mapper/UserMapper.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.mapper;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.RegisterResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserProfileResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.User;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapper;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapping;\\n\\n@Mapper(componentModel = \\\"spring\\\")\\npublic interface UserMapper {\\n\\n    RegisterResponse toRegisterResponse(User user);\\n\\n    @Mapping(target = \\\"userId\\\", source = \\\"id\\\")\\n    @Mapping(target = \\\"status\\\", expression = \\\"java(user.getStatus().name())\\\")\\n    UserProfileResponse toProfileResponse(User user);\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/mapper/UserSettingsMapper.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.mapper;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.PreferenceSelectionResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserSettingsResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.PreferenceOption;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserGenrePreference;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserSettings;\\nimport java.util.Comparator;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapper;\\n\\n@Mapper(componentModel = \\\"spring\\\")\\npublic interface UserSettingsMapper {\\n\\n    default UserSettingsResponse toResponse(UserSettings settings) {\\n        PreferenceSelectionResponse city = toSelection(settings.getPreferredCityOption());\\n        List<PreferenceSelectionResponse> genres = settings.getGenrePreferences().stream()\\n            .map(UserGenrePreference::getPreferenceOption)\\n            .sorted(Comparator.comparingLong(PreferenceOption::getId))\\n            .map(this::toSelection)\\n            .toList();\\n\\n        return new UserSettingsResponse(\\n            settings.getFullName(),\\n            settings.getPhone(),\\n            settings.getDob(),\\n            settings.getAddress(),\\n            city,\\n            genres,\\n            settings.isNotificationOptIn()\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    default PreferenceSelectionResponse toSelection(PreferenceOption option) {\\n        if (option == null) {\\n            return null;\\n        }\\n        return new PreferenceSelectionResponse(option.getId(), option.getValue());\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/mapper/PreferenceOptionMapper.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.mapper;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.PreferenceOptionItemResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.PreferenceOption;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapper;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapping;\\n\\n@Mapper(componentModel = \\\"spring\\\")\\npublic interface PreferenceOptionMapper {\\n\\n    @Mapping(target = \\\"type\\\", expression = \\\"java(option.getType().name())\\\")\\n    PreferenceOptionItemResponse toItemResponse(PreferenceOption option);\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/mapper/UserWalletMapper.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.mapper;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserWalletResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserWallet;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapper;\\nimport org.mapstruct.Mapping;\\n\\n@Mapper(componentModel = \\\"spring\\\")\\npublic interface UserWalletMapper {\\n\\n    @Mapping(target = \\\"balance\\\", source = \\\"balance.amount\\\")\\n    @Mapping(target = \\\"currency\\\", source = \\\"balance.currency\\\")\\n    UserWalletResponse toResponse(UserWallet wallet);\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_VyvU0tfgMl91BNE5pLE3FOkE"
  }
}

## Entry 223
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:18:43.538Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:18:43.538Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/service/AuthService.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.service;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.LoginRequest;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.PasswordChangeRequest;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.PasswordResetConfirmRequest;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.RegisterRequest;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.AccessTokenResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.AuthTokensResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.MessageResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.RegisterResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserProfileResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.PasswordResetToken;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.RefreshToken;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.User;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserWallet;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.exception.IdentityException;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.exception.UserNotFoundException;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.mapper.UserMapper;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.PasswordResetTokenRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.RefreshTokenRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserWalletRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.ValidationException;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.JwtTokenProvider;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.SecurityConstants;\\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\\nimport java.security.MessageDigest;\\nimport java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;\\nimport java.security.SecureRandom;\\nimport java.time.Instant;\\nimport java.util.Base64;\\nimport java.util.Locale;\\nimport java.util.Optional;\\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\\nimport org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;\\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\\n\\n@Service\\npublic class AuthService {\\n\\n    private static final SecureRandom SECURE_RANDOM = new SecureRandom();\\n\\n    private final UserRepository userRepository;\\n    private final UserWalletRepository userWalletRepository;\\n    private final RefreshTokenRepository refreshTokenRepository;\\n    private final PasswordResetTokenRepository passwordResetTokenRepository;\\n    private final PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder;\\n    private final JwtTokenProvider jwtTokenProvider;\\n    private final UserMapper userMapper;\\n\\n    public AuthService(\\n        UserRepository userRepository,\\n        UserWalletRepository userWalletRepository,\\n        RefreshTokenRepository refreshTokenRepository,\\n        PasswordResetTokenRepository passwordResetTokenRepository,\\n        PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder,\\n        JwtTokenProvider jwtTokenProvider,\\n        UserMapper userMapper\\n    ) {\\n        this.userRepository = userRepository;\\n        this.userWalletRepository = userWalletRepository;\\n        this.refreshTokenRepository = refreshTokenRepository;\\n        this.passwordResetTokenRepository = passwordResetTokenRepository;\\n        this.passwordEncoder = passwordEncoder;\\n        this.jwtTokenProvider = jwtTokenProvider;\\n        this.userMapper = userMapper;\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public RegisterResponse register(RegisterRequest request) {\\n        request.validateNoUnknownFields();\\n\\n        String normalizedEmail = normalizeEmail(request.getEmail());\\n        if (userRepository.existsByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizedEmail)) {\\n            throw new IdentityException(\\\"Email already in use\\\", \\\"DUPLICATE_EMAIL\\\", HttpStatus.CONFLICT);\\n        }\\n\\n        User user = new User(normalizedEmail, passwordEncoder.encode(request.getPassword()));\\n        User savedUser = userRepository.save(user);\\n        userWalletRepository.save(new UserWallet(savedUser));\\n        return userMapper.toRegisterResponse(savedUser);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\n    public AuthTokensResponse login(LoginRequest request) {\\n        User user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizeEmail(request.email()))\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new IdentityException(\\\"Invalid credentials\\\", \\\"INVALID_CREDENTIALS\\\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED));\\n\\n        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.password(), user.getPasswordHash())) {\\n            throw new IdentityException(\\\"Invalid credentials\\\", \\\"INVALID_CREDENTIALS\\\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED);\\n        }\\n\\n        String accessToken = jwtTokenProvider.generateToken(user.getId(), user.getRole());\\n        String refreshTokenRaw = generateOpaqueToken();\\n        String refreshTokenHash = hashToken(refreshTokenRaw);\\n\\n        RefreshToken refreshToken = new RefreshToken(\\n            user,\\n            refreshTokenHash,\\n            Instant.now().plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_S)\\n        );\\n        refreshTokenRepository.save(refreshToken);\\n\\n        return new AuthTokensResponse(accessToken, refreshTokenRaw);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public AccessTokenResponse refreshToken(String refreshTokenRaw) {\\n        RefreshToken refreshToken = refreshTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(refreshTokenRaw))\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new IdentityException(\\\"Refresh token invalid\\\", \\\"REFRESH_TOKEN_INVALID\\\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED));\\n\\n        if (refreshToken.isRevoked() || refreshToken.isExpired(Instant.now())) {\\n            throw new IdentityException(\\\"Refresh token invalid\\\", \\\"REFRESH_TOKEN_INVALID\\\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED);\\n        }\\n\\n        String accessToken = jwtTokenProvider.generateToken(refreshToken.getUser().getId(), refreshToken.getUser().getRole());\\n        return new AccessTokenResponse(accessToken);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse logout(String refreshTokenRaw) {\\n        Optional<RefreshToken> refreshTokenOpt = refreshTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(refreshTokenRaw));\\n        refreshTokenOpt.ifPresent(token -> {\\n            token.revoke(Instant.now());\\n            refreshTokenRepository.save(token);\\n        });\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Logged out successfully\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\n    public UserProfileResponse me(Long userId) {\\n        User user = userRepository.findById(userId).orElseThrow(() -> new UserNotFoundException(userId));\\n        return userMapper.toProfileResponse(user);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse changePassword(Long userId, PasswordChangeRequest request) {\\n        User user = userRepository.findById(userId).orElseThrow(() -> new UserNotFoundException(userId));\\n\\n        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.currentPassword(), user.getPasswordHash())) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Current password incorrect\\\", \\\"CURRENT_PASSWORD_INCORRECT\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        if (!request.newPassword().equals(request.confirmPassword())) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Password mismatch\\\", \\\"PASSWORD_MISMATCH\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        user.changePassword(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));\\n        userRepository.save(user);\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Password changed successfully\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse requestPasswordReset(String email) {\\n        Optional<User> userOptional = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizeEmail(email));\\n        if (userOptional.isPresent()) {\\n            User user = userOptional.get();\\n            passwordResetTokenRepository.invalidateActiveByUserId(user.getId(), Instant.now());\\n            PasswordResetToken token = new PasswordResetToken(\\n                user,\\n                hashToken(generateOpaqueToken()),\\n                Instant.now().plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S)\\n            );\\n            passwordResetTokenRepository.save(token);\\n        }\\n\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Password reset token generated\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse confirmPasswordReset(PasswordResetConfirmRequest request) {\\n        if (!request.newPassword().equals(request.confirmPassword())) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Password mismatch\\\", \\\"PASSWORD_MISMATCH\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        PasswordResetToken token = passwordResetTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(request.token()))\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new ValidationException(\\\"Reset token invalid\\\", \\\"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\\\"));\\n\\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\\n        if (token.isConsumed() || token.isExpired(now)) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Reset token invalid\\\", \\\"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        PasswordResetToken latestToken = passwordResetTokenRepository.findTopByUserIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc(token.getUser().getId())\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new ValidationException(\\\"Reset token invalid\\\", \\\"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\\\"));\\n\\n        if (!latestToken.getId().equals(token.getId())) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Reset token invalid\\\", \\\"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        User user = token.getUser();\\n        user.changePassword(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));\\n        userRepository.save(user);\\n\\n        token.consume(now);\\n        passwordResetTokenRepository.save(token);\\n\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Password reset successfully\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    private String normalizeEmail(String email) {\\n        return email.trim().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);\\n    }\\n\\n    private String generateOpaqueToken() {\\n        byte[] bytes = new byte[32];\\n        SECURE_RANDOM.nextBytes(bytes);\\n        return Base64.getUrlEncoder().withoutPadding().encodeToString(bytes);\\n    }\\n\\n    private String hashToken(String token) {\\n        try {\\n            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance(\\\"SHA-256\\\");\\n            byte[] encoded = digest.digest(token.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));\\n            StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder(encoded.length * 2);\\n            for (byte b : encoded) {\\n                builder.append(String.format(\\\"%02x\\\", b));\\n            }\\n            return builder.toString();\\n        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException ex) {\\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"SHA-256 algorithm unavailable\\\", ex);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/service/UserSettingsService.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.service;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.UserSettingsUpsertRequest;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserSettingsResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.PreferenceOption;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.User;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserSettings;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.enums.PreferenceOptionType;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.exception.UserNotFoundException;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.mapper.UserSettingsMapper;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.PreferenceOptionRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserSettingsRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.ValidationException;\\nimport java.util.LinkedHashSet;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport java.util.Set;\\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\\n\\n@Service\\npublic class UserSettingsService {\\n\\n    private final UserSettingsRepository userSettingsRepository;\\n    private final UserRepository userRepository;\\n    private final PreferenceOptionRepository preferenceOptionRepository;\\n    private final UserSettingsMapper userSettingsMapper;\\n\\n    public UserSettingsService(\\n        UserSettingsRepository userSettingsRepository,\\n        UserRepository userRepository,\\n        PreferenceOptionRepository preferenceOptionRepository,\\n        UserSettingsMapper userSettingsMapper\\n    ) {\\n        this.userSettingsRepository = userSettingsRepository;\\n        this.userRepository = userRepository;\\n        this.preferenceOptionRepository = preferenceOptionRepository;\\n        this.userSettingsMapper = userSettingsMapper;\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\n    public UserSettingsResponse getMySettings(Long userId) {\\n        UserSettings settings = userSettingsRepository.findWithPreferencesByUserId(userId)\\n            .orElseGet(() -> new UserSettings(getUserOrThrow(userId)));\\n        return userSettingsMapper.toResponse(settings);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public UserSettingsResponse upsertMySettings(Long userId, UserSettingsUpsertRequest request) {\\n        validateGenres(request.preferredGenreOptionIds());\\n\\n        User user = getUserOrThrow(userId);\\n        UserSettings settings = userSettingsRepository.findWithPreferencesByUserId(userId)\\n            .orElseGet(() -> new UserSettings(user));\\n\\n        PreferenceOption cityOption = preferenceOptionRepository\\n            .findByIdAndTypeAndActiveTrue(request.preferredCityOptionId(), PreferenceOptionType.CITY)\\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new ValidationException(\\\"Invalid preference\\\", \\\"INVALID_PREFERENCE\\\"));\\n\\n        Set<Long> genreIds = new LinkedHashSet<>(request.preferredGenreOptionIds());\\n        List<PreferenceOption> genreOptions = preferenceOptionRepository\\n            .findByIdInAndTypeAndActiveTrue(genreIds, PreferenceOptionType.GENRE);\\n\\n        if (genreOptions.size() != genreIds.size()) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Invalid preference\\\", \\\"INVALID_PREFERENCE\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        settings.updateProfile(\\n            request.fullName(),\\n            request.phone(),\\n            request.dob(),\\n            request.address(),\\n            cityOption,\\n            request.notificationOptIn()\\n        );\\n token.revoke(Instant.now());\\n            refreshTokenRepository.save(token);\\n        });\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Logged out successfully\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\\n    public UserProfileResponse me(Long userId) {\\n        User user = userRepository.findById(userId).orElseThrow(() -> new UserNotFoundException(userId));\\n        return userMapper.toProfileResponse(user);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse changePassword(Long userId, PasswordChangeRequest request) {\\n        User user = throw new ValidationException(\\\"Current password incorrect\\\", \\\"CURRENT_PASSWORD_INCORRECT\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        if (!request.newPassword().equals(request.confirmPassword())) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Password mismatch\\\", \\\"PASSWORD_MISMATCH\\\");\\n        }\\n\\n        user.changePassword(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));\\n        userRepository.save(user);\\n        return new MessageResponse(\\\"Password changed successfully\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Transactional\\n    public MessageResponse requestPasswordReset(String email) hashToken(generateOpaqueToken()),\\n                Instant.now().plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S)\\n            );\\n            passwordResetTokenRepository.save(token);\\n \\\"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\\\"));\\n\\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\\n        if (token.isConsumed() || token.isExpired(now)) {\\n            throw new ValidationException(\\\"Reset token org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ResponseStatus;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/auth\\\")\\npublic class AuthController {\\n\\n    private final AuthService authService;\\n\\n    public AuthController(AuthService authService) {\\n        this.authService = authService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/register\\\")\\n    @ResponseStatus(HttpStatus.CREATED)\\n    public RegisterResponse register(@Valid @RequestBody RegisterRequest request) {\\n        return authService.register(request);\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/login\\\")\\n    public AuthTokensResponse login(@Valid @RequestBody LoginRequest request) {\\n        return authService.login(request);\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/token/refresh\\\")\\n    public AccessTokenResponse refresh(@Valid @RequestBody RefreshTokenRequest request) {\\n        return authService.refreshToken(request.refreshToken());\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/logout\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public MessageResponse logout(@Valid @RequestBody LogoutRequest request) {\\n        return authService.logout(request.refreshToken());\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/me\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public UserProfileResponse me(Authentication authentication) {\\n        return authService.me(currentUserId(authentication));\\n    }\\n\\n @PostMapping(\\\"/password/reset\\\")\\n    public MessageResponse requestPasswordReset(@Valid @RequestBody PasswordResetRequest request) {\\n        return authService.requestPasswordReset(request.email());\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/password/reset/confirm\\\")\\n    public MessageResponse confirmPasswordReset(@Valid @RequestBody PasswordResetConfirmRequest request) {\\n        return authService.confirmPasswordReset(request);\\n    }\\n\\n    private Long currentUserId(Authentication authentication) {\\n        return (Long) ## Entry 238
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:19:47.540Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:19:47.540Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V10__create_users.sql <<'EOF'\\n-- owner: identity module\\nCREATE TABLE users (\\n    id            BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\\n    email         VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\n    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\n    role          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USER',\\n    status        VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',\\n    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\\n    updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\\n    CONSTRAINT chk_user_role CHECK (role IN ('USER', 'ADMIN')),\\n    CONSTRAINT chk_user_status CHECK (status IN ('ACTIVE', 'INACTIVE'))\\n);\\n\\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uk_users_email_lower ON users (LOWER(email));\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V11__create_preference_options.sql <<'EOF'\\n-- owner: identity module\\nCREATE TABLE preference_options (\\n    id         BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\\n    type       VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,\\n    value      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,\\n    active     BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,\\n    sort_order INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\\n    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\\n    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\\n    CONSTRAINT chk_preference_type CHECK (type IN ('CITY', 'GENRE')),\\n    CONSTRAINT uk_preference_type_value UNIQUE (type, value)\\n);\\n\\nCREATE INDEX idx_preference_type ON preference_options (type);\\nCREATE INDEX idx_preference_active ON preference_options (active);\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V12__create_user_settings.sql <<'EOF'\\n-- owner: identity module\\nCREATE TABLE user_settings (\\n    id                       BIGSERIAL    PRIMARY KEY,\\n    user_id                  BIGINT       NOT NULL,\\n    full_name                VARCHAR(255),\\n    phone                    VARCHAR(20),\\n    dob                      DATE,\\n ## Entry 288
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:22:08.671Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:22:08.671Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Implemented `SPEC_3.MD` onboarding/auth flow in the `identity` module, plus Flyway schema for FR3.\n\n**What I built**\n- Full identity domain + persistence model:\n  - `User`, `UserSettings`, `UserWallet`, `PreferenceOption`, `UserGenrePreference`, `RefreshToken`, `PasswordResetToken`\n  - Enums: `UserStatus`, `PreferenceOptionType`\n  - Repositories for all required aggregates\n- Auth + onboarding services:\n  - `AuthService` (register/login/refresh/logout/me/change-password/reset-request/reset-confirm)\n  - `UserSettingsService`, `PreferenceOptionsService`, `UserWalletService`\n  - `UserDetailsServiceImpl` for Spring Security authentication wiring\n- API layer (as in spec):\n  - `AuthController`\n  - `UserSettingsController`\n  - `PreferenceOptionsController`\n  - `UserWalletController`\n  - Request/response DTOs under `identity/api/dto/...`\n- MapStruct mappers:\n  - `UserMapper`, `UserSettingsMapper`, `PreferenceOptionMapper`, `UserWalletMapper`\n- Exceptions:\n  - `IdentityException`, `UserNotFoundException`\n\n**Migrations added**\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V10__create_users.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V11__create_preference_options.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V12__create_user_settings.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V13__create_user_genre_preferences.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V14__create_user_wallets.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V15__create_refresh_tokens.sql`\n- `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V16__create_password_reset_tokens.sql`\n\n**Build/dependency updates**\n- Updated `identity/pom.xml` with JPA, Security, and MapStruct dependencies/processors.\n\n**Verification**\n- Ran compile successfully:\n  - `mvn -pl identity -am -DskipTests compile`\n  - Also verified broader reactor compile (`identity,app`) succeeds.\n\nIf you want, I can now implement the Stage 8 test suite for this SPEC (`domain/service/api/arch` layers).",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

}

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 302
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:24:44.498Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:24:44.498Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_4HIWzKfQfZZmuiIkeroKG5K0",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 5dfbb2\nWall time: 0.0515 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1285\nOutput:\n# TEST_AGENT.md — Testing Standards & Execution Agent\n\n> Read this file before writing or running tests.\n> This agent is responsible for test code implementation, execution, failure fixing, and result logging.\n\n---\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis file defines the responsibilities of the **Test Agent**.\n\nThe **SPEC_GENERATOR** is the sole source of truth for what to test — it writes all test cases in Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file. The **TEST_AGENT does not decide what to test**. Its only job is to:\n\n- **Translate** the test cases written in `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8 into executable test code\n- **Run** those tests\n- **Fix** failures caused by new or changed code\n- **Log** results in a timestamped report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Scope Ownership\n\n### Test Agent MUST\n\n- Read Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file and implement exactly those test cases — nothing more, nothing less\n- Write test code for all layers as specified in the test cases:\n  - `domain/` (pure unit)\n  - `service/` (Mockito orchestration)\n  - `api/` (`@WebMvcTest` HTTP contract)\n  - `arch/` (ArchUnit boundaries)\n- Run relevant tests after implementation\n- Fix failing tests and related test setup issues\n- Fix production code only when required to satisfy approved spec behavior\n- Log all found errors and all applied fixes in a test report file\n\n### Test Agent MUST NOT\n\n- Decide what test cases to write — that is SPEC_GENERATOR's job\n- Add, remove, or modify test cases from Stage 8\n- Introduce unapproved features not present in `SPEC_*.md`\n- Change module ownership or dependency direction rules\n- Add module-level `@ControllerAdvice`\n- Skip failed tests without documenting reason and impact\n\n---\n\n## Inputs (Required Before Writing Tests)\n\n1. Confirmed feature spec with Stage 8 test cases: `SPEC_*.md` ← **primary input**\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then full module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All Stage 8 test cases from `SPEC_*.md` are implemented as code\n- All implemented tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test — writes all test cases in Stage 8\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` writes test code for Stage 8 cases, runs them, fixes failures, and publishes the final test report"
  }
}
```

Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. ## Entry 303
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:24:44.499Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:24:44.499Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_ZmNuGVhROEcK3JebQ1M4m2kx",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ba9544\nWall time: 0.0555 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2631\nOutput:\n# Testing Guide — Modular Monolith\n\n> Tests are not optional. Every feature must have coverage across all four layers.\n> The `arch/` tests in particular are self-enforcing — they fail the build automatically\n> if anyone violates a module boundary. Never delete or weaken an arch test.\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle — Each Layer Tests One Thing\n\nThe most common testing mistake is putting the wrong test in the wrong layer.\nUse this rule: **each layer tests exactly one concern.**\n\n| Layer | Tests One Concern | Spring Context? | Key Annotation |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `domain/` | Business rules on the domain object | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n| `service/` | Orchestration — right calls in right order | None | `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` |\n| `api/` | HTTP contract — status codes, JSON shape, validation | Web layer only | `@WebMvcTest` |\n| `arch/` | Architectural boundaries | None | `@AnalyzeClasses` |\n| `mapper/` | Field translation correctness | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n\n---\n\n## Test Folder Structure\n\nEvery module must mirror its source structure exactly:\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/\n    ├── main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    │   ├── api/controller/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   ├── service/\n    │   ├── repository/\n    │   ├── mapper/\n    │   └── event/\n    └── test/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n        ├── domain/        ← Pure unit tests. No Spring. No infrastructure mocks.\n        ├── service/       ← Mockito only. Mocks repositories and ACL facades.\n        ├── api/           ← @WebMvcTest slice. Mocks service and mapper.\n        ├── arch/          ← ArchUnit. Enforces module boundary rules at build time.\n        └── mapper/        ← MapStruct translation tests. No Spring.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Test Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\nAll test methods must follow this exact pattern:\n\n```\nshould_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]\n```\n\nThe name must be readable as a sentence describing the behaviour being verified.\nNever use vague names like `testConfirm()`, `test1()`, or `happyPath()`.\n\n**Good examples:**\n```\nshould_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending\nshould_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking\nshould_return_200_when_booking_is_found\nshould_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum\nshould_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist\nshould_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_after_successful_confirmation\nshould_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_id_is_unknown\nshould_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_status\n```\n\n**Bad examples (never use):**\n```\ntestConfirm()\ntest_happy_path()\nconfirmWorks()\nbooking_test_1()\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 1 — `domain/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the domain object enforces its own rules correctly.\nThese are the fastest tests in the suite — they must run in milliseconds.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- Valid state transitions succeed and produce the correct new state\n- Invalid state transitions throw `BusinessRuleException` with the correct message\n- Guard conditions (preconditions on methods) are enforced\n- Value object construction validates its invariants\n- Terminal states cannot be transitioned out of\n- Null or invalid inputs are rejected\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Persistence (no repositories, no DB)\n- HTTP (no controllers, no MockMvc)\n- Spring beans (no `@SpringBootTest`, no `@ExtendWith(SpringExtension.class)`)\n- Mocks of infrastructure — if you need a mock here, the domain has an infrastructure\n  dependency. Fix the domain, not the test.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Plain JUnit 5 only.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — no annotations on the class\nclass BookingTest {\n    ...\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — domain tests never load Spring\n@SpringBootTest\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n\n// ❌ Wrong — if you need this, your domain imports infrastructure\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest that the domain method succeeds and produces the correct outcome under valid conditions.\nCover every valid state transition defined in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Happy path: valid transition succeeds\n    @Test\n    void should_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        // Arrange — set up the entity in a valid starting state\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        // Act — call the domain method\n        booking.confirm();\n\n        // Assert — verify the expected outcome\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_confirmed_and_event_is_more_than_48h_away() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusDays(3);\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\nTest that invalid operations are rejected with the correct exception and message.\nCover every forbidden transition in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Negative path: invalid transition throws BusinessRuleException\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_an_already_confirmed_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Booking is already confirmed\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_cancelling_within_48h_of_event() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusHours(24); // inside the 48h window\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Exception / Edge Case Tests\n\nTest boundary conditions, null inputs, minimum/maximum values, and terminal states.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_booking_id_is_null() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(null, BookingStatus.PENDING))\n            .isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING, 9))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot book more than 8 seats\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_operating_on_a_terminal_cancelled_state() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        // All operations on a terminal state must be rejected\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 2 — `service/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the service correctly orchestrates domain objects, repositories, ACL facades,\nand event publishing. These tests prove the *use case* works — not the domain rule, not the\nHTTP contract.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- The correct repository method is called with the correct arguments\n- The correct ACL facade method is called when an external system is involved\n- `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` is called with the correct event type and payload\n- `BookingNotFoundException` (or equivalent) is thrown when the entity is not found\n- `BusinessRuleException` propagates correctly when the domain rejects an operation\n- `[System]IntegrationException` is thrown (and compensation is triggered) when an external call fails\n- No event is published when the use case fails\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Domain rules — those are tested in `domain/`. Do not re-test `booking.confirm()` logic here.\n- HTTP concerns — no MockMvc, no status codes.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) // Mockito only — no Spring\nclass BookingServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private BookingRepository bookingRepository;     // Always mock the repository\n\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher; // Always mock the event publisher\n\n    @Mock\n    private PaymentAclService paymentAclService;      // Mock ACL facades for external systems\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private BookingService bookingService;            // The class under test — real instance\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest the complete use case: entity is found, domain method succeeds, repository is saved,\nevent is published, ACL facade is called if required.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_confirm_booking_and_publish_event_when_booking_is_pending() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    // Assert — verify orchestration\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(booking);                               // saved\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingConfirmedEvent.class)); // event fired\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_initiate_booking_and_call_payment_facade_when_request_is_valid() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenAnswer(inv -> inv.getArgument(0));\n\n    // Act\n    Booking result = bookingService.initiateBooking(request);\n\n    // Assert\n"
  }
}

assertThat(result.slot().getLastSyncError()).isNotNull();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_updating_PENDING_SYNC_slot() {\n        ShowSlot slot = baseSlot();\n        slot.markPendingSync();\n        when(showSlotRepository.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(slot));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> showSlotService.updateSlot(1L, 1L, new UpdateShowSlotRequest(\"New\", null, null, null, null, null)))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_transition_to_CANCELLED_and_call_cancelEvent_when_slot_is_ACTIVE() {\n        ShowSlot slot = baseSlot();\n        slot.markActive();\n        slot.setEbEventId(\"eb-1\");\n        when(showSlotRepository.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(slot));\n        doAnswer(invocation -> {\n            slot.markCancelled();\n            return null;\n        }).when(stateMachineService).sendEvent(slot, ShowSlotEvent.CANCEL);\n\n        boolean result = showSlotService.cancelSlot(1L, 1L);\n\n---\npackage com.eventplatform.scheduling.arch;\n\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.AnalyzeClasses;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.ArchTest;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.lang.ArchRule;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ControllerAdvice;\n\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.noClasses;\n\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.eventplatform.scheduling\")\nclass SchedulingArchTest {\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule should_not_have_controller_advice =\n        noClasses().should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class);\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule should_not_depend_on_downstream_modules =\n        noClasses()\n            .that().resideInAPackage(\"..scheduling..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat()\n            .resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..com.eventplatform.bookinginventory..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.promotions..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.engagement..\",\n                \"..com.eventplatform.admin..\"\n            );\n}\n"
  }
}
```

org.mockito.Mockito.when;\nimport static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.request.MockMvcRequestBuilders.get;\nimport static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.status;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.controller.CityCatalogController;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.response.CityListResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.response.CityResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.mapper.VenueMapper;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.CityCatalogService;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.EventCatalogService;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.VenueService;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.VenueCatalogService;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired;\nimport org.springframework.boot.test.autoconfigure.web.servlet.AutoConfigureMockMvc;\nimport org.springframework.boot.test.context.SpringBootTest;\nimport org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean;\nimport org.springframework.test.web.servlet.MockMvc;\n\n@SpringBootTest(\n    classes = com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.DiscoveryCatalogTestApplication.class,\n    properties = {\n        \"app.default-org-id=1\",\n        \"spring.autoconfigure.exclude=org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.DataSourceAutoConfiguration,\" +\n            \"org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.orm.jpa.HibernateJpaAutoConfiguration\"\n    }\n)\n@AutoConfigureMockMvc(addFilters = false)\nclass CityCatalogControllerTest {\n\n    @Autowired\n    private MockMvc mockMvc;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private CityCatalogService cityCatalogService;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private EventCatalogService eventCatalogService;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private VenueCatalogService venueCatalogService;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private VenueService venueService;\n\n    @MockBean\n    private VenueMapper venueMapper;\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_200_when_cities_listed() throws Exception {\n        when(cityCatalogService.listCities(1L))\n            .thenReturn(new CityListResponse(List.of(new CityResponse(1L, \"NYC\", null, null, null, null, null)), 1));\n\n        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/v1/catalog/cities\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isOk());\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

## Entry 322
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T07:26:09.631Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T07:26:09.631Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_d9iGf2GwIWAgDdy0iReEkWAF",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/domain/UserSettings.java\n@@\n package com.eventplatform.identity.domain;\n \n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\n import jakarta.persistence.CascadeType;\n@@\n import java.time.LocalDate;\n import java.util.ArrayList;\n+import java.util.HashSet;\n import java.util.List;\n+import java.util.Set;\n@@\n     public void updateProfile(\n         String fullName,\n         String phone,\n         LocalDate dob,\n         String address,\n         PreferenceOption preferredCityOption,\n         boolean notificationOptIn\n     ) {\n+        if (preferredCityOption == null) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+        }\n         this.fullName = fullName;\n         this.phone = phone;\n         this.dob = dob;\n         this.address = address;\n         this.preferredCityOption = preferredCityOption;\n         this.notificationOptIn = notificationOptIn;\n     }\n \n     public void replaceGenrePreferences(List<PreferenceOption> genreOptions) {\n+        if (genreOptions.size() > 3) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+        }\n+        Set<Long> uniqueIds = new HashSet<>();\n+        for (PreferenceOption option : genreOptions) {\n+            if (!uniqueIds.add(option.getId())) {\n+                throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+            }\n+        }\n         genrePreferences.clear();\n         for (PreferenceOption genreOption : genreOptions) {\n             genrePreferences.add(new UserGenrePreference(this, genreOption));\n         }\n     }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

{

    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: identity/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/identity/domain/UserSettings.java\n@@\n package com.eventplatform.identity.domain;\n \n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\n import jakarta.persistence.CascadeType;\n@@\n import java.time.LocalDate;\n import java.util.ArrayList;\n+import java.util.HashSet;\n import java.util.List;\n+import java.util.Set;\n@@\n     public void updateProfile(\n         String fullName,\n         String phone,\n         LocalDate dob,\n         String address,\n         PreferenceOption preferredCityOption,\n         boolean notificationOptIn\n     ) {\n+        if (preferredCityOption == null) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+        }\n         this.fullName = fullName;\n         this.phone = phone;\n         this.dob = dob;\n         this.address = address;\n         this.preferredCityOption = preferredCityOption;\n         this.notificationOptIn = notificationOptIn;\n     }\n \n     public void replaceGenrePreferences(List<PreferenceOption> genreOptions) {\n+        if (genreOptions.size() > 3) {\n+            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+        }\n+        Set<Long> uniqueIds = new HashSet<>();\n+        for (PreferenceOption option : genreOptions) {\n+            if (!uniqueIds.add(option.getId())) {\n+                throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n+            }\n+        }\n         genrePreferences.clear();\n         for (PreferenceOption genreOption : genreOptions) {\n             genrePreferences.add(new UserGenrePreference(this, genreOption));\n         }\n     }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
